WHY FLASH LEGAL FUNDING

## Why Plaintiffs and Attorneys Choose Us

**Non-Recourse — Zero Risk**

Our funding is non-recourse, meaning if you lose your case, you owe us nothing. The risk is entirely on us, not you.

**No Credit Check Required**

Your credit score doesn't matter. Approval is based entirely on the strength of your personal injury case and expected settlement value.

**Cash Within 24 Hours**

Once approved, we can send your pre-settlement cash advance within 24 hours. No waiting weeks or months for financial relief.

**No Monthly Payments**

Unlike traditional loans, there are no monthly installments. Repayment comes from your settlement proceeds only after your case resolves.

**Competitive Rates**

As a direct funder — not a broker — we offer some of the lowest rates in the pre-settlement funding industry. No middleman markups.

**Attorney-Friendly Process**

We work directly with your law firm to evaluate your case quickly and professionally, without interfering with your legal strategy.

OUR STORY

## Our Story

Flash Legal Funding was founded in 2022 in Los Angeles, California to provide non-recourse pre-settlement funding for personal injury plaintiffs and their attorneys. Our cash advances help injured clients pay bills, cover rent, and manage medical expenses while their personal injury case is still pending — giving attorneys the time they need to negotiate the best possible settlement. By offering a risk-free, non-recourse funding solution with no credit checks and no monthly payments, we help plaintiffs avoid the financial pressure that leads to premature, low-value settlements.

WHAT IS IT?

## What Is Non-Recourse Pre-Settlement Funding?

Pre-settlement funding — also known as a lawsuit cash advance or litigation financing — provides financial support to personal injury plaintiffs who are waiting for their case to settle or reach a judgment. A funding company advances a portion of the expected settlement amount so you can cover living expenses, medical bills, rent, and other costs while your case is resolved.

Because this funding is **non-recourse**, repayment comes only from your settlement proceeds. If your personal injury case does not result in a recovery, you owe nothing. This makes pre-settlement funding fundamentally different from a traditional loan — there is no personal liability, no credit check, and no monthly payments.

Your browser does not support the video tag.

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS

## Pre-Settlement Funding FAQ

### What is non-recourse pre-settlement funding?

Non-recourse pre-settlement funding is a cash advance provided to personal injury plaintiffs while their lawsuit is pending. Unlike a loan, it is repaid only from your settlement proceeds. If your case is unsuccessful, you owe nothing — the funding company absorbs the loss. There are no credit checks, no monthly payments, and no personal liability.

### Do I need good credit to get lawsuit funding?

No. Pre-settlement funding approval is based entirely on the strength and projected value of your personal injury case — not your credit score, employment status, or income. We never run a credit check as part of our application process.

### How much pre-settlement funding can I get?

Most plaintiffs receive between 10-20% of the estimated settlement value of their case. The exact amount depends on the type and severity of your injuries, the clarity of liability, your medical treatment costs, and the available insurance coverage. We work with your attorney to determine an appropriate funding amount.

### What happens if I lose my case after receiving funding?

You owe nothing. Because pre-settlement funding is non-recourse, repayment comes only from your settlement proceeds. If your personal injury case does not result in a settlement or judgment, you keep the money and have zero obligation to repay. The risk is entirely on the funding company.

### How fast can I get a pre-settlement cash advance?

Many applicants receive funding within 24 hours of approval. After you apply and we review your case details with your attorney, funds can be sent the same day via bank transfer, check, or wire. The entire process from application to funding can take as little as one business day.

# Non-Recourse Pre-Settlement Funding FAQ | Personal Injury Questions | Flash Legal Funding

> Frequently asked questions about non-recourse pre-settlement funding for personal injury cases. Learn about the application process, no credit check policy, repayment terms, eligible case types, and why you owe nothing if you lose.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/faq/

# Pre-Settlement Funding FAQ

Answers to common questions about non-recourse pre-settlement funding for personal injury cases. If you don’t see your question, [contact us](/contact/).

No credit check Non-recourse Attorney cooperation required

Pre-settlement funding is a non-recourse cash advance against the potential value of a pending personal injury lawsuit. It helps plaintiffs cover living expenses, medical bills, rent, and other costs while their case is still in progress. Unlike a traditional loan, you only repay from your settlement proceeds — and if you lose, you owe nothing.

No, it is not a traditional loan. Pre-settlement funding is a **non-recourse cash advance**, meaning repayment is contingent on the outcome of your personal injury case. There are no monthly payments, no credit checks, and no personal liability. If your case does not result in a recovery, you owe nothing.

**Non-recourse** means the funding company can only collect repayment from your lawsuit settlement proceeds. If your personal injury case does not result in a recovery, you owe nothing. The risk is on the funding company, not you — making pre-settlement funding fundamentally different from a bank loan or credit card debt.

No. Pre-settlement funding approval is based entirely on the merits of your personal injury case — not your credit score, employment status, or income. We evaluate the strength of your claim, liability, and insurance coverage.

In most situations, yes. We typically coordinate with your attorney/law firm to request case documents and confirm key details.

Timing depends on how quickly we receive the needed case information from the law firm. Once we have it, decisions can be made quickly.

Amounts vary based on case type, stage, liability, damages, insurance coverage, and other underwriting factors. We can provide a range after initial review.

We provide non-recourse pre-settlement funding for a wide range of personal injury cases, including:

- Car accident injuries
- Truck and commercial vehicle accidents
- Motorcycle accident injuries
- Slip and fall / premises liability
- Workplace injury claims
- Pedestrian and bicycle accidents
- Rideshare accidents (Uber, Lyft)
- Other personal injury lawsuits (evaluated case-by-case)

Because funding is generally non-recourse, if there is no recovery you may owe nothing, subject to the terms of your agreement.

Your attorney remains in control of legal strategy. Funding is designed to help you avoid financial pressure while your case progresses. Any impact depends on your specific situation and your agreement’s terms.

We typically request information from your attorney such as claim details, insurance information, and key documents relevant to evaluating liability and damages.

In some cases, additional funding may be available later depending on case progress and updated information. Additional advances require review and approval.

If your case resolves with a recovery, repayment is typically made from the settlement proceeds at the time of disbursement, per the agreement.

We do not charge an application fee. Your agreement will explain all terms and any costs associated with the advance.

Yes. Use our online form on the [Apply](/apply/) page. You can also call [(859) 81-FLASH](tel:+18598135274).

If representation changes, we may need updated contact information and continued cooperation from the new firm so the funding can be properly managed.

The terms "lawsuit loan" and "pre-settlement funding" are often used interchangeably, but technically pre-settlement funding is a **non-recourse cash advance** — not a loan. Unlike a traditional loan, there is no personal obligation to repay if you lose your case. Flash Legal Funding provides true non-recourse funding for personal injury plaintiffs.

Yes. Car accident cases are one of the most common types of personal injury cases we fund. If you have been injured in a car accident and have an attorney representing you, you may qualify for a non-recourse cash advance from Flash Legal Funding. [Apply online](/apply/) to get started.

Insurance companies know that injured plaintiffs under financial pressure are more likely to accept low-ball settlement offers. Pre-settlement funding covers your bills so you can afford to wait for a fair offer. This "holding power" gives your attorney the time needed to negotiate the maximum value of your personal injury case.

Still have questions? Call [(859) 81-FLASH](tel:+18598135274) or email [info@flashlegalfunding.com](mailto:info@flashlegalfunding.com).

# Pre-Settlement Funding for Attorneys & Law Firms | Flash Legal Funding

> Partner with Flash Legal Funding to offer non-recourse pre-settlement funding to your personal injury clients. Streamlined process, competitive rates, and financially stable clients who can wait for maximum case value.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/for-attorneys/

[Home](/) › **For Attorneys**

# Pre-Settlement Funding for Attorneys & Law Firms

Financially stable clients are patient clients. Patient clients let you fight for maximum case value. Partner with Flash Legal Funding to give your personal injury clients the support they need.

[Contact Our Team](/contact/)

## Why Attorneys Partner with Flash Legal Funding

Insurance companies use a simple strategy: **Delay, Deny, Defend.** They know that financially desperate plaintiffs are more likely to accept low-ball settlement offers. When your clients can't pay rent or afford medical treatment, they pressure you to settle quickly — even when the case is worth significantly more.

Pre-settlement funding neutralizes this tactic. When your clients have the financial stability to wait, you gain the leverage to negotiate from a position of strength and pursue the full value of their claims.

## How It Benefits Your Practice

**Higher Case Values**

Clients who can afford to wait don't accept low-ball offers. Better settlements mean better outcomes for your clients and your firm.

**Fewer Desperate Calls**

Financially stable clients don't call every week asking when the case will settle. Funding reduces the emotional pressure that slows your practice down.

**Better Medical Treatment**

Clients with funding continue their medical treatment without gaps. Complete medical records strengthen the case and increase its value.

**Streamlined Process**

We require minimal documentation from your office. Our team handles the underwriting and keeps the process efficient and professional.

## Our Process for Attorneys

- **Your client applies** online or by phone — takes just a few minutes
- **We contact your office** to request basic case information (demand letter, medical records, police report as applicable)
- **We underwrite the case** and determine funding eligibility and amount
- **Client receives funds** — typically within 24 hours of approval
- **Repayment at settlement** — we are repaid from the client's portion of the settlement proceeds at case resolution

We never interfere with your legal strategy, communicate with opposing counsel, or make decisions about the case. Our role is strictly financial support for your client.

## What Makes Flash Legal Funding Different

- **Direct funder:** We underwrite and fund cases ourselves — no brokers shopping your client's case to multiple companies
- **Competitive rates:** Fair, transparent pricing without hidden fees or compounding surprises
- **Non-recourse:** Your client owes nothing if the case is unsuccessful
- **Minimal burden on your staff:** We handle the process efficiently with minimal documentation requests
- **Professional communication:** We treat your office with the same professionalism you expect from any business partner

## Case Types We Fund

We provide pre-settlement funding for a wide range of personal injury cases:

- [Car accident cases](/car-accident-funding/)
- [Truck accident cases](/truck-accident-funding/)
- [Motorcycle accident cases](/motorcycle-accident-funding/)
- [Slip and fall / premises liability](/slip-and-fall-funding/)
- [Workplace injury cases](/workplace-injury-funding/)
- Rideshare and pedestrian accident cases
- Other personal injury claims

Have questions about how pre-settlement funding works for your practice? [Contact us](/contact/) or call [(859) 81-FLASH](tel:+18598135274).

## Partner with Flash Legal Funding

Give your personal injury clients the financial stability they need so you can fight for the settlements they deserve.

[Contact Us to Get Started](/contact/)

# Car Accident Pre-Settlement Funding | Flash Legal Funding

> Get non-recourse pre-settlement funding for your car accident case. No credit check, no monthly payments. If you lose your case, you owe $0. Apply today.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/car-accident-funding/

[Home](/) › **Car Accident Funding**

# Car Accident Pre-Settlement Funding

Injured in a car accident? Get a non-recourse cash advance while your attorney fights for the settlement you deserve. No credit check, no monthly payments, and no risk.

[Apply Now — Takes 2 Minutes](/apply/)

## How Car Accident Pre-Settlement Funding Works

Car accident lawsuits can take months or even years to settle. During that time, you may be dealing with mounting medical bills, lost wages, vehicle repairs, and everyday living expenses. Pre-settlement funding from Flash Legal Funding provides a cash advance against your expected settlement so you can stay financially stable while your case moves forward.

Because our funding is **non-recourse**, you only repay from your settlement proceeds. If your car accident case is unsuccessful, you owe nothing — we absorb the loss entirely.

## Who Qualifies for Car Accident Funding?

To be eligible for pre-settlement funding on a car accident case, you generally need:

- A pending car accident personal injury lawsuit or claim
- Legal representation from an attorney or law firm
- Documented injuries and medical treatment
- Clear or strong liability in the accident

We do not check your credit score, employment status, or income. Approval is based entirely on the merits and projected value of your car accident case.

## Common Car Accident Injuries We Fund

We provide pre-settlement cash advances for all types of car accident injuries, including:

- Whiplash and soft tissue injuries
- Broken bones and fractures
- Traumatic brain injuries (TBI)
- Spinal cord injuries and herniated discs
- Internal organ damage
- Burns and scarring

## Why Choose Flash Legal Funding for Your Car Accident Case?

- **No risk:** Non-recourse means if you lose your case, you keep the money and owe $0
- **No credit check:** Your credit score is never a factor in our decision
- **Fast funding:** Receive your cash advance within 24 hours of approval
- **Competitive rates:** We're a direct funder, not a broker — no middleman markups
- **Attorney-friendly:** We work professionally with your legal team without interfering with your case strategy

Don't let financial pressure force you into accepting a low-ball settlement offer from the insurance company. Pre-settlement funding gives you the breathing room to let your attorney fight for the full value of your car accident claim.

Ready to apply? [Start your application](/apply/) or call us at [(859) 81-FLASH](tel:+18598135274).

## Car Accident Funding FAQ

### How much pre-settlement funding can I get for a car accident case?

Funding amounts are based on the estimated value of your car accident case. Most plaintiffs receive between 10-20% of the expected settlement value. Factors include the severity of your injuries, liability clarity, medical treatment costs, and available insurance coverage.

### Do I need a lawyer to get pre-settlement funding for my car accident?

Yes, in most cases you need legal representation. We coordinate with your attorney to review case documents and evaluate the strength of your car accident claim. This helps us determine your eligibility and funding amount.

### What happens if I lose my car accident lawsuit after receiving funding?

Because our funding is non-recourse, you owe nothing if your case is unsuccessful. The risk is entirely on us. If your car accident lawsuit does not result in a settlement or judgment, you keep the money and have no obligation to repay.

### How fast can I receive car accident pre-settlement funding?

Many applicants receive funding within 24 hours of approval. Once we review your case details with your attorney and approve your application, funds can be sent via bank transfer, check, or wire the same day.

# Truck Accident Pre-Settlement Funding | Flash Legal Funding

> Get non-recourse pre-settlement funding for your truck accident case. No credit check, no monthly payments. Covers medical bills and living expenses while you wait for your settlement.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/truck-accident-funding/

[Home](/) › **Truck Accident Funding**

# Truck Accident Pre-Settlement Funding

Truck accident lawsuits involve severe injuries and lengthy litigation. Get a non-recourse cash advance to cover your expenses while your attorney fights for full compensation.

[Apply Now — Takes 2 Minutes](/apply/)

## Why Truck Accident Victims Need Pre-Settlement Funding

Truck accident cases are among the most complex personal injury claims. They often involve multiple liable parties — the truck driver, trucking company, vehicle manufacturer, and cargo loaders — along with federal safety regulations and massive insurance policies. This complexity means your case may take significantly longer to resolve than a standard car accident claim.

During this time, you may be unable to work due to catastrophic injuries while medical bills continue to pile up. Pre-settlement funding from Flash Legal Funding bridges this financial gap so you don't have to accept a low-ball settlement out of desperation.

## How Truck Accident Funding Works

Our process is straightforward and designed to get you cash quickly:

- **Apply online** or call us — takes just a few minutes
- **We review your case** with your attorney to evaluate the claim's strength and estimated value
- **Get approved and funded** — many applicants receive funds within 24 hours of approval

Because our funding is **non-recourse**, repayment comes only from your settlement proceeds. If your truck accident case does not result in a recovery, you owe us absolutely nothing.

## Common Truck Accident Injuries We Fund

- Traumatic brain injuries (TBI) and concussions
- Spinal cord injuries and paralysis
- Multiple fractures and crushed limbs
- Internal organ damage and internal bleeding
- Severe burns and disfigurement
- Wrongful death claims (surviving family members)

## Why Choose Flash Legal Funding?

- **Higher funding amounts:** Truck accident settlements are typically larger, which means more funding may be available to you
- **No risk:** Non-recourse funding means you owe $0 if you lose
- **No credit check:** Approval is based on your case, not your credit
- **Direct funder:** We're not a broker — you get competitive rates without middleman markups
- **Attorney-friendly:** We coordinate professionally with your legal team

Ready to apply? [Start your application](/apply/) or call us at [(859) 81-FLASH](tel:+18598135274).

## Truck Accident Funding FAQ

### Why do truck accident cases take so long to settle?

Truck accident cases are often more complex than typical auto accidents. They involve multiple liable parties (trucking company, driver, manufacturer), federal trucking regulations, extensive discovery, and higher settlement values. Insurance companies and their attorneys aggressively defend these cases, which can extend litigation for years.

### How much pre-settlement funding can I get for a truck accident case?

Because truck accident settlements tend to be larger due to the severity of injuries and liability factors, funding amounts can be higher than other case types. We typically advance 10-20% of the estimated case value. The exact amount depends on your injuries, liability, medical treatment, and insurance coverage available.

### Is truck accident pre-settlement funding a loan?

No. Pre-settlement funding is a non-recourse cash advance, not a loan. Repayment comes only from your settlement proceeds if you win. If your truck accident case is unsuccessful, you owe nothing. There are no credit checks, no monthly payments, and no personal liability.

# Motorcycle Accident Pre-Settlement Funding | Flash Legal Funding

> Get non-recourse pre-settlement funding for your motorcycle accident case. No credit check, no monthly payments. Cover medical bills and living expenses while your case settles.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/motorcycle-accident-funding/

[Home](/) › **Motorcycle Accident Funding**

# Motorcycle Accident Pre-Settlement Funding

Motorcycle accidents cause devastating injuries with long recovery times. Get a non-recourse cash advance so you can focus on healing while your attorney handles your case.

[Apply Now — Takes 2 Minutes](/apply/)

## Why Motorcycle Accident Victims Need Pre-Settlement Funding

Motorcycle accidents often result in far more severe injuries than car accidents. Without the protective frame of a vehicle, riders are exposed to direct impact, road rash, and ejection injuries. Recovery from these injuries can take months or years, leaving you unable to work and facing mounting bills.

Making matters worse, insurance companies frequently try to lowball motorcycle accident claims — or shift blame onto the rider. Your attorney needs time to build a strong case and negotiate for full compensation. Pre-settlement funding from Flash Legal Funding gives you the **financial breathing room** to wait for the settlement you deserve, instead of accepting a fraction of your claim's value out of desperation.

## Common Motorcycle Accident Injuries We Fund

- Road rash and skin grafts
- Broken bones and compound fractures
- Traumatic brain injuries (TBI)
- Spinal cord injuries and paralysis
- Limb amputations
- Internal organ damage
- PTSD and psychological trauma

## How It Works

- **Apply in minutes:** Submit your application online or call us
- **Quick case review:** We work with your attorney to evaluate your motorcycle accident claim
- **Get funded fast:** Approved applicants can receive cash within 24 hours

Our funding is **non-recourse** — you only repay from your settlement if you win. If your motorcycle accident case is unsuccessful, you keep the cash advance and owe us nothing.

## Why Choose Flash Legal Funding?

- **Non-recourse — zero risk:** Lose your case? You owe $0
- **No credit check:** We evaluate your case, not your credit history
- **Fast funding:** Cash within 24 hours of approval
- **Direct funder:** No broker fees — competitive rates from the source
- **Use funds freely:** Medical bills, rent, groceries, vehicle replacement — your choice

Don't let financial pressure force you into a lowball settlement. [Apply now](/apply/) or call [(859) 81-FLASH](tel:+18598135274).

## Motorcycle Accident Funding FAQ

### Can I get pre-settlement funding after a motorcycle accident?

Yes. If you have a pending motorcycle accident personal injury lawsuit and legal representation, you may qualify for non-recourse pre-settlement funding. We evaluate your case based on liability, injury severity, and available insurance coverage — not your credit score.

### Why are motorcycle accident settlements often larger?

Motorcycle riders lack the structural protection of a car, which means motorcycle accidents frequently result in more severe injuries — broken bones, road rash, spinal injuries, and traumatic brain injuries. More serious injuries typically mean higher medical costs, longer recovery times, and greater settlement values.

### Will the insurance company's bias against motorcyclists affect my funding?

No. We evaluate your case objectively based on the merits — liability, documented injuries, and medical treatment. We understand that insurance companies sometimes try to shift blame to motorcyclists. Our underwriting focuses on the facts of your case, not biases.

[What Is an MVA?](/posts/what-is-a-mva/) | [How to Choose the Right Company](/posts/how-to-choose-the-right-company/) | [How Interest Rates Are Calculated](/posts/how-interest-rates-are-calculated/)

## Other Case Types We Fund

# Slip and Fall Pre-Settlement Funding | Flash Legal Funding

> Get non-recourse pre-settlement funding for your slip and fall case. No credit check, no monthly payments. Cover medical bills and expenses while your premises liability lawsuit is pending.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/slip-and-fall-funding/

[Home](/) › **Slip and Fall Funding**

# Slip and Fall Pre-Settlement Funding

Injured in a slip and fall accident? Get non-recourse funding to cover medical bills and living expenses while your premises liability case is pending. No credit check, no risk.

[Apply Now — Takes 2 Minutes](/apply/)

## Pre-Settlement Funding for Slip and Fall Injuries

Slip and fall accidents caused by hazardous conditions on someone else's property can result in serious injuries — broken bones, head trauma, back injuries, and more. These premises liability cases often require extensive medical treatment and time away from work, creating significant financial strain while you wait for a settlement.

Flash Legal Funding provides **non-recourse pre-settlement cash advances** for slip and fall cases so you can focus on your recovery instead of worrying about bills. If your case is unsuccessful, you owe us nothing.

## Common Slip and Fall Scenarios We Fund

- Wet or slippery floors in stores, restaurants, or public buildings
- Uneven sidewalks, broken stairs, or damaged flooring
- Poorly lit parking lots or stairwells
- Ice and snow accumulation on commercial property
- Missing handrails or guardrails
- Cluttered aisles or obstructed walkways

## How Slip and Fall Funding Works

Our process is simple and fast:

- **Apply online or by phone** — provide basic case details and your attorney's contact information
- **We review your case** — we coordinate with your attorney to evaluate liability, damages, and insurance coverage
- **Get funded fast** — approved applicants can receive cash within 24 hours

No credit check. No employment verification. No monthly payments. Repayment comes only from your settlement proceeds if you win your case.

## Why Slip and Fall Victims Choose Flash Legal Funding

- **Zero risk:** Non-recourse funding — if you lose, you owe $0
- **No upfront costs:** No application fees or hidden charges
- **Fast approval:** Cash in as little as 24 hours
- **Direct funder:** Competitive rates with no broker middlemen
- **Use funds for anything:** Rent, medical bills, groceries, car payments — whatever you need

Don't let financial pressure force you to settle your slip and fall case for less than it's worth. [Apply now](/apply/) or call [(859) 81-FLASH](tel:+18598135274).

## Slip and Fall Funding FAQ

### Can I get pre-settlement funding for a slip and fall case?

Yes. Flash Legal Funding provides non-recourse cash advances for slip and fall premises liability cases. If you've been injured due to a property owner's negligence and have an attorney handling your case, you may qualify for funding.

### What do I need to prove in a slip and fall case to get funding?

We evaluate your case based on liability (did the property owner know about or create the hazardous condition?), the severity of your injuries and medical treatment, and available insurance coverage. Strong cases with clear negligence and documented injuries are most likely to be approved.

### How long do slip and fall lawsuits typically take?

Slip and fall cases can take anywhere from several months to over two years to settle, depending on the complexity of liability, severity of injuries, and whether the case goes to trial. Pre-settlement funding helps bridge this financial gap so you can wait for a fair settlement.

# Workplace Injury Pre-Settlement Funding | Flash Legal Funding

> Get non-recourse pre-settlement funding for your workplace injury case. No credit check, no monthly payments. If you lose your case, you owe $0. Apply today.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/workplace-injury-funding/

[Home](/) › **Workplace Injury Funding**

# Workplace Injury Pre-Settlement Funding

Hurt on the job and waiting for your lawsuit to settle? Get a non-recourse cash advance to cover bills and living expenses. No credit check, no monthly payments, no risk.

[Apply Now — Takes 2 Minutes](/apply/)

## Financial Relief While Your Workplace Injury Case Is Pending

Workplace injuries can be devastating — not just physically, but financially. When you're unable to work due to an injury caused by a third party's negligence, the bills don't stop coming. Rent, medical expenses, groceries, and other essentials still need to be paid while your personal injury lawsuit slowly moves through the legal system.

Flash Legal Funding provides **non-recourse pre-settlement cash advances** for workplace injury cases. This means you get money now, and repayment comes only from your settlement proceeds. If you lose your case, you owe us nothing.

## Types of Workplace Injury Cases We Fund

- Construction site accidents (falls, collapses, struck-by incidents)
- Industrial machinery and equipment injuries
- Toxic chemical or substance exposure
- Warehouse and forklift accidents
- Scaffolding and ladder falls
- Electrical injuries and burns
- Repetitive stress and overexertion injuries

## Pre-Settlement Funding vs. Workers' Compensation

It's important to understand the difference. **Workers' compensation** is a no-fault insurance system that covers medical bills and partial lost wages regardless of who caused the injury. **Pre-settlement funding** applies to personal injury lawsuits filed against third parties responsible for your injury — such as a negligent contractor, equipment manufacturer, or property owner.

If you have a third-party personal injury claim in addition to a workers' comp claim, you may be eligible for pre-settlement funding on the personal injury portion of your case.

## Why Choose Flash Legal Funding?

- **Non-recourse — zero risk:** If you lose your case, you keep the money and owe nothing
- **No credit check:** Approval based entirely on your case merits
- **Fast funding:** Cash within 24 hours of approval
- **Direct funder:** Competitive rates — no broker fees or middlemen
- **Covers any expense:** Use funds for rent, medical bills, groceries, car payments — whatever you need

[Apply now](/apply/) or call us at [(859) 81-FLASH](tel:+18598135274) to get started.

## Workplace Injury Funding FAQ

### Can I get pre-settlement funding for a workplace injury?

Yes, if you have filed a personal injury lawsuit related to a workplace injury and have legal representation, you may qualify for non-recourse pre-settlement funding. This applies to third-party liability claims where someone other than your employer is responsible for your injury.

### Is workplace injury pre-settlement funding different from workers' compensation?

Yes. Pre-settlement funding applies to personal injury lawsuits filed against third parties, not to workers' compensation claims. For example, if you were injured by defective equipment made by a third-party manufacturer, or hurt at a construction site due to another contractor's negligence, you may file a personal injury lawsuit in addition to any workers' comp claim.

### What types of workplace injuries qualify for funding?

We fund a wide range of workplace injury cases including construction site accidents, industrial machinery injuries, toxic exposure, falling objects, scaffolding collapses, warehouse accidents, and more. The key factors are clear liability against a third party, documented injuries, and active legal representation.

## Related Reading

[How Flash Helps](/posts/how-flash-helps/) | [How Funding Increases Case Value](/posts/how-funding-increases-case-value/) | [Is a Lawsuit Loan Legal?](/posts/is-a-lawsuit-loan-legal/)

[Car Accident](/car-accident-funding/) [Truck Accident](/truck-accident-funding/) [Motorcycle Accident](/motorcycle-accident-funding/) [Slip and Fall](/slip-and-fall-funding/) [Medical Malpractice](/medical-malpractice-funding/) [Dog Bite](/dog-bite-funding/) [Pedestrian Accident](/pedestrian-accident-funding/) [Rideshare Accident](/rideshare-accident-funding/) [Wrongful Death](/wrongful-death-funding/) [Product Liability](/product-liability-funding/)

## Get Funded for Your Workplace Injury Case

No credit check. No monthly payments. No risk. Apply in minutes and get funded within 24 hours of approval.

[Apply for Workplace Injury Funding](/apply/)

# Medical Malpractice Pre-Settlement Funding | Flash Legal Funding

> Get non-recourse pre-settlement funding for your medical malpractice case. No credit check, no monthly payments. If you lose your case, you owe $0. Apply today.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/medical-malpractice-funding/

[Home](/) › **Medical Malpractice Funding**

# Medical Malpractice Pre-Settlement Funding

Injured by a healthcare provider's negligence? Get a non-recourse cash advance while your attorney builds your medical malpractice case. No credit check, no monthly payments, and no risk.

[Apply Now — Takes 2 Minutes](/apply/)

## How Medical Malpractice Pre-Settlement Funding Works

Medical malpractice lawsuits are among the most complex and time-consuming personal injury cases. They often require extensive expert testimony, medical record review, and multiple depositions — and they can take years to resolve. During that time, you may be facing mounting medical bills, lost income, and ongoing treatment costs.

Pre-settlement funding from Flash Legal Funding provides a cash advance against your expected medical malpractice settlement so you can cover living expenses, medical care, and other costs while your case moves through litigation.

Because our funding is **non-recourse**, you only repay from your settlement proceeds. If your medical malpractice case is unsuccessful, you owe nothing — we absorb the loss entirely.

## Who Qualifies for Medical Malpractice Funding?

To be eligible for pre-settlement funding on a medical malpractice case, you generally need:

- A pending medical malpractice lawsuit or claim
- Legal representation from an attorney experienced in malpractice litigation
- Documented injuries caused by medical negligence
- Evidence supporting a breach of the standard of care

We do not check your credit score, employment status, or income. Approval is based entirely on the merits and projected value of your medical malpractice case.

## Common Medical Malpractice Cases We Fund

We provide pre-settlement cash advances for all types of medical malpractice injuries, including:

- Surgical errors and wrong-site surgery
- Misdiagnosis or delayed diagnosis
- Birth injuries (cerebral palsy, Erb's palsy, brain damage)
- Medication and prescription errors
- Anesthesia errors
- Emergency room negligence
- Failure to diagnose cancer
- Nursing home abuse and neglect

## Why Choose Flash Legal Funding for Your Medical Malpractice Case?

- **No risk:** Non-recourse means if you lose your case, you keep the money and owe $0
- **No credit check:** Your credit score is never a factor in our decision
- **Fast funding:** Receive your cash advance within 24 hours of approval
- **Competitive rates:** We're a direct funder, not a broker — no middleman markups
- **Attorney-friendly:** We work professionally with your legal team without interfering with your case strategy

Medical malpractice cases often result in substantial settlements, but the wait can be financially devastating. Pre-settlement funding gives you the breathing room to let your attorney build the strongest possible case without settling for less than you deserve.

Ready to apply? [Start your application](/apply/) or call us at [(859) 81-FLASH](tel:+18598135274).

## Medical Malpractice Funding FAQ

### How much pre-settlement funding can I get for a medical malpractice case?

Funding amounts depend on the estimated value of your medical malpractice case. Most plaintiffs receive between 10-20% of the expected settlement value. Factors include the severity of the injury, the strength of your malpractice claim, medical costs, and long-term care needs.

### Why do medical malpractice cases take so long to settle?

Medical malpractice claims involve complex medical evidence, expert witness testimony, and often face aggressive defense from hospital insurance carriers. Cases can take two to five years or longer, which is why pre-settlement funding is especially helpful for malpractice plaintiffs who need financial support during litigation.

### What happens if I lose my medical malpractice case after receiving funding?

Because our funding is non-recourse, you owe nothing if your case is unsuccessful. If your medical malpractice lawsuit does not result in a settlement or verdict, you keep the money and have no obligation to repay.

### What types of medical malpractice cases qualify for pre-settlement funding?

We fund a wide range of medical malpractice cases including surgical errors, misdiagnosis, delayed diagnosis, birth injuries, medication errors, anesthesia mistakes, and failure to treat. As long as you have legal representation and a viable claim, you may qualify for funding.

# Dog Bite Pre-Settlement Funding | Flash Legal Funding

> Get non-recourse pre-settlement funding for your dog bite injury case. No credit check, no monthly payments. If you lose your case, you owe $0. Apply today.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/dog-bite-funding/

[Home](/) › **Dog Bite Funding**

# Dog Bite Pre-Settlement Funding

Suffered a serious dog bite or animal attack? Get a non-recourse cash advance while your attorney pursues your personal injury claim. No credit check, no monthly payments, and no risk.

[Apply Now — Takes 2 Minutes](/apply/)

## How Dog Bite Pre-Settlement Funding Works

Dog bite injuries can be physically and emotionally devastating — and the medical bills can add up quickly. Reconstructive surgery, infection treatment, physical therapy, and psychological counseling are common expenses that victims face while waiting for their case to resolve.

Pre-settlement funding from Flash Legal Funding provides a cash advance against your expected dog bite settlement so you can cover medical costs, lost wages, and daily living expenses while your attorney negotiates with the dog owner's insurance company.

Because our funding is **non-recourse**, you only repay from your settlement proceeds. If your dog bite case is unsuccessful, you owe nothing — we absorb the loss entirely.

## Who Qualifies for Dog Bite Funding?

To be eligible for pre-settlement funding on a dog bite case, you generally need:

- A pending dog bite or animal attack personal injury claim
- Legal representation from an attorney or law firm
- Documented injuries requiring medical treatment
- An identifiable liable party with insurance coverage

We do not check your credit score, employment status, or income. Approval is based entirely on the merits and projected value of your dog bite case.

## Common Dog Bite Injuries We Fund

We provide pre-settlement cash advances for all types of dog bite and animal attack injuries, including:

- Deep puncture wounds and lacerations
- Facial injuries and disfigurement
- Nerve damage and loss of function
- Infections (including rabies treatment)
- Broken bones from knockdowns
- Scarring requiring reconstructive surgery
- Emotional trauma and PTSD

## Why Choose Flash Legal Funding for Your Dog Bite Case?

- **No risk:** Non-recourse means if you lose your case, you keep the money and owe $0
- **No credit check:** Your credit score is never a factor in our decision
- **Fast funding:** Receive your cash advance within 24 hours of approval
- **Competitive rates:** We're a direct funder, not a broker — no middleman markups
- **Attorney-friendly:** We work professionally with your legal team without interfering with your case strategy

Dog bite cases can take months to settle, especially when serious injuries or disputed liability are involved. Pre-settlement funding gives you the financial stability to wait for a fair settlement instead of accepting a low offer.

Ready to apply? [Start your application](/apply/) or call us at [(859) 81-FLASH](tel:+18598135274).

## Dog Bite Funding FAQ

### Can I get pre-settlement funding for a dog bite case?

Yes. Dog bite and animal attack cases are eligible for non-recourse pre-settlement funding. As long as you have an attorney and a pending personal injury claim, you can apply for a cash advance against your expected settlement.

### How much funding can I receive for a dog bite injury?

Funding amounts are based on the estimated value of your dog bite case. Most plaintiffs receive between 10-20% of the expected settlement. Factors include the severity of your injuries, medical treatment costs, scarring or disfigurement, and the dog owner's insurance coverage.

### What if the dog owner doesn't have insurance?

Many dog bite claims are covered under the dog owner's homeowner's or renter's insurance policy. Your attorney can help identify available coverage. If there is a viable path to recovery, you may still qualify for pre-settlement funding.

### Do I have to repay the funding if I lose my dog bite case?

No. Our funding is non-recourse, meaning you owe nothing if your case does not result in a settlement or judgment. The financial risk is entirely on us, not on you.

# Pedestrian Accident Pre-Settlement Funding | Flash Legal Funding

> Get non-recourse pre-settlement funding for your pedestrian accident case. No credit check, no monthly payments. If you lose your case, you owe $0. Apply today.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/pedestrian-accident-funding/

[Home](/) › **Pedestrian Accident Funding**

# Pedestrian Accident Pre-Settlement Funding

Hit by a vehicle while walking or crossing the street? Get a non-recourse cash advance while your attorney fights for your personal injury settlement. No credit check, no monthly payments, and no risk.

[Apply Now — Takes 2 Minutes](/apply/)

## How Pedestrian Accident Pre-Settlement Funding Works

Pedestrian accidents often result in severe, life-altering injuries. Without the protection of a vehicle, pedestrians struck by cars, trucks, or buses frequently suffer catastrophic harm — and the recovery process can take months or years. During that time, medical bills pile up, income is lost, and everyday expenses don't stop.

Pre-settlement funding from Flash Legal Funding provides a cash advance against your expected pedestrian accident settlement so you can stay financially stable while your case moves through negotiation or litigation.

Because our funding is **non-recourse**, you only repay from your settlement proceeds. If your pedestrian accident case is unsuccessful, you owe nothing — we absorb the loss entirely.

## Who Qualifies for Pedestrian Accident Funding?

To be eligible for pre-settlement funding on a pedestrian accident case, you generally need:

- A pending pedestrian accident personal injury lawsuit or claim
- Legal representation from an attorney or law firm
- Documented injuries and ongoing medical treatment
- Clear or strong liability on the part of the driver

We do not check your credit score, employment status, or income. Approval is based entirely on the merits and projected value of your pedestrian accident case.

## Common Pedestrian Accident Injuries We Fund

We provide pre-settlement cash advances for all types of pedestrian accident injuries, including:

- Traumatic brain injuries (TBI)
- Spinal cord injuries and paralysis
- Broken bones and fractures (pelvis, legs, arms)
- Internal organ damage
- Knee and joint injuries
- Road rash and soft tissue damage
- Wrongful death claims filed by surviving family

## Why Choose Flash Legal Funding for Your Pedestrian Accident Case?

- **No risk:** Non-recourse means if you lose your case, you keep the money and owe $0
- **No credit check:** Your credit score is never a factor in our decision
- **Fast funding:** Receive your cash advance within 24 hours of approval
- **Competitive rates:** We're a direct funder, not a broker — no middleman markups
- **Attorney-friendly:** We work professionally with your legal team without interfering with your case strategy

Don't let financial pressure force you into accepting a low settlement offer. Pedestrian accident cases often have high value due to the severity of injuries — pre-settlement funding gives you the time to pursue full compensation.

Ready to apply? [Start your application](/apply/) or call us at [(859) 81-FLASH](tel:+18598135274).

## Pedestrian Accident Funding FAQ

### Can pedestrian accident victims get pre-settlement funding?

Yes. If you were hit by a vehicle as a pedestrian and have a pending personal injury claim with legal representation, you can apply for non-recourse pre-settlement funding to cover expenses while your case is resolved.

### How much funding can I get for a pedestrian accident case?

Funding amounts are based on the estimated value of your pedestrian accident case. Most plaintiffs receive between 10-20% of the expected settlement value. Pedestrian accidents often involve serious injuries, which can mean higher settlement values and larger funding amounts.

### What if I was partially at fault for the pedestrian accident?

You may still qualify for funding even if you share some fault. Many states follow comparative negligence rules that allow you to recover damages even with partial fault. We evaluate each case individually with your attorney to determine eligibility.

### How fast can I receive funding for my pedestrian accident case?

Many applicants receive funding within 24 hours of approval. Once we review your case details with your attorney and approve your application, funds can be sent via bank transfer, check, or wire the same day.

# Rideshare Accident Pre-Settlement Funding (Uber & Lyft) | Flash Legal Funding

> Get non-recourse pre-settlement funding for your Uber, Lyft, or rideshare accident case. No credit check, no monthly payments. If you lose your case, you owe $0.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/rideshare-accident-funding/

[Home](/) › **Rideshare Accident Funding**

# Rideshare Accident Pre-Settlement Funding

Injured in an Uber, Lyft, or other rideshare accident? Get a non-recourse cash advance while your attorney navigates the complex insurance claims. No credit check, no monthly payments, and no risk.

[Apply Now — Takes 2 Minutes](/apply/)

## How Rideshare Accident Pre-Settlement Funding Works

Rideshare accidents involving Uber, Lyft, and similar services present unique legal challenges. Multiple insurance policies may apply depending on the driver's status at the time of the accident, and disputes between insurers can significantly delay your settlement.

Pre-settlement funding from Flash Legal Funding provides a cash advance against your expected rideshare accident settlement so you can cover medical bills, lost wages, and living expenses while your attorney sorts through the insurance coverage and negotiates your claim.

Because our funding is **non-recourse**, you only repay from your settlement proceeds. If your rideshare accident case is unsuccessful, you owe nothing — we absorb the loss entirely.

## Who Qualifies for Rideshare Accident Funding?

To be eligible for pre-settlement funding on a rideshare accident case, you generally need:

- A pending rideshare accident personal injury lawsuit or claim
- Legal representation from an attorney or law firm
- Documented injuries and medical treatment
- An identifiable liable party (rideshare driver, third-party driver, or rideshare company)

We do not check your credit score, employment status, or income. Approval is based entirely on the merits and projected value of your rideshare accident case.

## Types of Rideshare Accident Claims We Fund

We provide pre-settlement cash advances for all types of rideshare-related injuries, including accidents where you were:

- A passenger in an Uber or Lyft vehicle
- A driver or passenger in another vehicle hit by a rideshare driver
- A pedestrian or cyclist struck by a rideshare vehicle
- A rideshare driver injured by another negligent driver

Common injuries include whiplash, broken bones, traumatic brain injuries, spinal cord injuries, and soft tissue damage.

## Why Choose Flash Legal Funding for Your Rideshare Accident Case?

- **No risk:** Non-recourse means if you lose your case, you keep the money and owe $0
- **No credit check:** Your credit score is never a factor in our decision
- **Fast funding:** Receive your cash advance within 24 hours of approval
- **Competitive rates:** We're a direct funder, not a broker — no middleman markups
- **Attorney-friendly:** We work professionally with your legal team without interfering with your case strategy

Rideshare accident cases can drag on for months while insurance companies fight over liability. Pre-settlement funding gives you financial stability so your attorney can pursue the maximum settlement without pressure to settle early.

Ready to apply? [Start your application](/apply/) or call us at [(859) 81-FLASH](tel:+18598135274).

## Rideshare Accident Funding FAQ

### Can I get pre-settlement funding for an Uber or Lyft accident?

Yes. If you were injured in a rideshare accident — whether as a passenger, another driver, or a pedestrian — and have a pending personal injury claim with legal representation, you can apply for non-recourse pre-settlement funding.

### Why are rideshare accident cases complicated?

Rideshare accident cases often involve multiple insurance policies — the driver's personal policy, the rideshare company's commercial policy, and potentially a third-party driver's coverage. Determining which policy applies depends on whether the driver was logged into the app, en route to a pickup, or actively transporting a passenger. This complexity can extend the timeline for settlement.

### How much funding can I receive for a rideshare accident case?

Funding amounts are based on the estimated value of your rideshare accident case. Most plaintiffs receive between 10-20% of the expected settlement. Uber and Lyft carry up to $1 million in liability coverage for active trips, which can support larger funding amounts for serious injuries.

### What happens if I lose my rideshare accident case after receiving funding?

Because our funding is non-recourse, you owe nothing if your case is unsuccessful. If your rideshare accident lawsuit does not result in a settlement or judgment, you keep the money and have no obligation to repay.

# Wrongful Death Pre-Settlement Funding | Flash Legal Funding

> Get non-recourse pre-settlement funding for your wrongful death lawsuit. No credit check, no monthly payments. If the case is unsuccessful, you owe $0. Apply today.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/wrongful-death-funding/

[Home](/) › **Wrongful Death Funding**

# Wrongful Death Pre-Settlement Funding

Lost a loved one due to someone else's negligence? Get a non-recourse cash advance to help your family through this difficult time while your attorney pursues justice. No credit check, no monthly payments, and no risk.

[Apply Now — Takes 2 Minutes](/apply/)

## How Wrongful Death Pre-Settlement Funding Works

Losing a family member to someone else's negligence is devastating — and the financial impact can be immediate. Funeral expenses, lost household income, mortgage payments, and childcare costs don't wait for a lawsuit to settle. Wrongful death cases often take years to resolve due to their complexity and the high stakes involved.

Pre-settlement funding from Flash Legal Funding provides a cash advance against the expected wrongful death settlement so surviving family members can manage expenses while the case moves through litigation.

Because our funding is **non-recourse**, repayment comes only from the settlement proceeds. If the wrongful death case is unsuccessful, you owe nothing — we absorb the loss entirely.

## Who Qualifies for Wrongful Death Funding?

To be eligible for pre-settlement funding on a wrongful death case, you generally need:

- A pending wrongful death lawsuit filed by an eligible family member or estate representative
- Legal representation from an attorney or law firm
- Evidence that the death was caused by another party's negligence or wrongful act
- A viable claim with identifiable liability and insurance coverage

We do not check your credit score, employment status, or income. Approval is based entirely on the merits and projected value of the wrongful death case.

## Common Wrongful Death Cases We Fund

We provide pre-settlement cash advances for wrongful death lawsuits arising from:

- Car, truck, and motorcycle accidents
- Medical malpractice and surgical errors
- Workplace accidents and construction site injuries
- Defective products and pharmaceutical liability
- Premises liability (unsafe property conditions)
- Nursing home neglect and abuse
- Pedestrian and bicycle accidents

## Why Choose Flash Legal Funding for Your Wrongful Death Case?

- **No risk:** Non-recourse means if the case is unsuccessful, you keep the money and owe $0
- **No credit check:** Your credit score is never a factor in our decision
- **Fast funding:** Receive your cash advance within 24 hours of approval
- **Competitive rates:** We're a direct funder, not a broker — no middleman markups
- **Compassionate service:** We understand the sensitivity of wrongful death cases and treat every family with care and respect

Wrongful death settlements can be substantial, but the legal process takes time. Pre-settlement funding ensures your family has the financial support to get through the waiting period without added stress.

Ready to apply? [Start your application](/apply/) or call us at [(859) 81-FLASH](tel:+18598135274).

## Wrongful Death Funding FAQ

### Can family members get pre-settlement funding for a wrongful death case?

Yes. If you are the surviving spouse, child, parent, or estate representative who has filed a wrongful death lawsuit with legal representation, you can apply for non-recourse pre-settlement funding to help cover immediate financial needs while the case is resolved.

### How much funding can I receive for a wrongful death case?

Funding amounts are based on the estimated value of the wrongful death case. Factors include the decedent's age, earning capacity, number of dependents, and the circumstances of the death. Wrongful death cases often carry substantial settlement values.

### What types of wrongful death cases qualify for funding?

We fund wrongful death cases arising from car accidents, truck accidents, medical malpractice, workplace accidents, defective products, premises liability, and other acts of negligence. As long as there is a viable claim and legal representation, the case may qualify.

### Do I have to repay the funding if the wrongful death case is lost?

No. Our funding is non-recourse, meaning you owe nothing if the case does not result in a settlement or verdict. The financial risk is entirely on us, not on you or your family.

# Product Liability Pre-Settlement Funding | Flash Legal Funding

> Get non-recourse pre-settlement funding for your product liability case. No credit check, no monthly payments. If you lose your case, you owe $0. Apply today.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/product-liability-funding/

[Home](/) › **Product Liability Funding**

# Product Liability Pre-Settlement Funding

Injured by a defective or dangerous product? Get a non-recourse cash advance while your attorney takes on the manufacturer. No credit check, no monthly payments, and no risk.

[Apply Now — Takes 2 Minutes](/apply/)

## How Product Liability Pre-Settlement Funding Works

Product liability lawsuits — cases against manufacturers, distributors, or retailers of defective products — are among the most complex and drawn-out types of personal injury litigation. They often involve corporate defendants with deep legal resources, making the fight for fair compensation a lengthy process.

Pre-settlement funding from Flash Legal Funding provides a cash advance against your expected product liability settlement so you can cover medical treatment, lost income, and daily expenses while your attorney builds your case against the product manufacturer.

Because our funding is **non-recourse**, you only repay from your settlement proceeds. If your product liability case is unsuccessful, you owe nothing — we absorb the loss entirely.

## Who Qualifies for Product Liability Funding?

To be eligible for pre-settlement funding on a product liability case, you generally need:

- A pending product liability lawsuit or claim
- Legal representation from an attorney experienced in product liability litigation
- Documented injuries caused by a defective product
- Evidence of a design defect, manufacturing defect, or failure to warn

We do not check your credit score, employment status, or income. Approval is based entirely on the merits and projected value of your product liability case.

## Types of Product Liability Cases We Fund

We provide pre-settlement cash advances for all types of defective product injuries, including:

- Defective medical devices (hip implants, surgical mesh, pacemakers)
- Dangerous pharmaceuticals and drug side effects
- Faulty automotive parts (tires, airbags, brakes)
- Defective consumer electronics
- Contaminated food and beverages
- Hazardous children's toys and products
- Defective industrial machinery and equipment
- Toxic chemical exposure from consumer products

## Why Choose Flash Legal Funding for Your Product Liability Case?

- **No risk:** Non-recourse means if you lose your case, you keep the money and owe $0
- **No credit check:** Your credit score is never a factor in our decision
- **Fast funding:** Receive your cash advance within 24 hours of approval
- **Competitive rates:** We're a direct funder, not a broker — no middleman markups
- **Attorney-friendly:** We work professionally with your legal team without interfering with your case strategy

Product liability cases can take years to resolve, especially when they involve mass tort or class action litigation. Pre-settlement funding gives you the financial cushion to wait for the compensation you deserve rather than settling for less.

Ready to apply? [Start your application](/apply/) or call us at [(859) 81-FLASH](tel:+18598135274).

## Product Liability Funding FAQ

### Can I get pre-settlement funding for a product liability case?

Yes. If you were injured by a defective or dangerous product and have a pending product liability lawsuit with legal representation, you can apply for non-recourse pre-settlement funding to cover expenses while your case is in progress.

### What types of product liability cases qualify for funding?

We fund cases involving defective medical devices, dangerous pharmaceuticals, faulty auto parts, defective consumer products, contaminated food, hazardous children's products, and defective industrial equipment. If a product caused you harm due to a design defect, manufacturing defect, or failure to warn, your case may qualify.

### Why do product liability cases take so long to settle?

Product liability cases often involve large corporations with well-funded legal teams. These cases require extensive expert analysis, product testing, regulatory review, and often proceed through multi-district litigation (MDL). This complexity can extend timelines to several years.

### What happens if I lose my product liability case after receiving funding?

Because our funding is non-recourse, you owe nothing if your case is unsuccessful. If your product liability lawsuit does not result in a settlement or verdict, you keep the money and have no obligation to repay.

# 5 Common Misconceptions About Lawsuit Funding | Flash Legal Funding

> Think you need good credit or a massive case to qualify? Think again. We debunk the biggest myths about pre-settlement funding.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/posts/5-common-misconceptions/

By Flash Legal Funding Editorial Team · Published March 20, 2026

Funding Basics

# 5 Common Misconceptions About Lawsuit Funding

Think you need good credit or a massive case to qualify? Think again. We debunk the biggest myths about pre-settlement funding.

March 20, 2026 • By Flash Legal Funding

Lawsuit funding (also known as pre-settlement funding) is an invaluable resource for plaintiffs struggling financially while they wait for their case to resolve. However, the industry is often misunderstood.

There are many myths surrounding legal funding that prevent people from getting the help they need. Today, we are clearing up five of the most common misconceptions.

## 1. Myth: It’s a Loan Fact: It’s a Non-Recourse Advance

This is the most common confusion. While it feels like a loan because you are receiving money now to pay back later, the legal structure is completely different. A loan must be repaid no matter what. Lawsuit funding is a **non-recourse cash advance**. This means repayment is contingent entirely on the outcome of your case. If you lose, you owe nothing. Zero risk.

## 2. Myth: You Need Good Credit to Qualify Fact: Your Credit Score Doesn't Matter

Traditional banks look at your credit score and employment history. We don't. At Flash Legal Funding, we are investing in the strength of your lawsuit, not your personal financial history. Whether you have perfect credit or no credit, your eligibility depends solely on the merits of your case and the likelihood of a settlement.

## 3. Myth: Lawsuit Funding is "Too Expensive" Fact: It Can Actually Help You Win More

While funding companies do charge fees, consider the alternative. Without funding, financial pressure might force you to accept the first low-ball offer the insurance company throws at you. Funding gives you the "holding power" to reject low offers and let your attorney fight for the maximum settlement. Often, the increase in your final settlement amount outweighs the cost of the funding.

## 4. Myth: It’s Only for Legal Expenses Fact: Use the Money for Whatever You Need

Some people assume this money must go toward attorney fees. That is incorrect (your attorney usually works on contingency anyway). This money is for **you**. You can use it to pay rent, cover medical bills, buy groceries, fix your car, or handle any other living expense. There are no restrictions on how you spend your advance.

## 5. Myth: It’s Only for Massive Class-Action Cases Fact: We Fund Cases of All Sizes

You don't need to be involved in a multi-million dollar class-action suit to qualify. Flash Legal Funding helps clients with all types of personal injury claims, including car accidents, slip and falls, and workplace injuries. No case is too small if you have a valid claim and a need for help.

## Conclusion

By dispelling these myths, we hope more plaintiffs feel empowered to use this financial tool. Lawsuit funding is a safe, regulated way to keep your life on track while your attorney does their job.

If you have more questions or want to see if you qualify, don't hesitate to reach out to us at Flash Legal Funding.

This article is for general informational purposes. Consult with your attorney regarding funding options in your state.

# Understanding the Benefits of Pre-Settlement Funding | Flash Legal Funding

> Explore how pre-settlement funding provides financial stability, covers living expenses, and levels the playing field during your lawsuit.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/posts/benefits-of-pre-settlement-funding/

By Flash Legal Funding Editorial Team · Published March 10, 2026

Funding Basics

# Understanding the Benefits of Pre-Settlement Funding

Explore how pre-settlement funding provides financial stability, covers living expenses, and levels the playing field during your lawsuit.

March 10, 2026 • By Flash Legal Funding

If you are in the middle of a personal injury lawsuit, you know that bills don't stop just because you are injured. You need financial room to breathe while your attorney fights for the settlement you deserve. Pre-settlement funding might be the exact solution you have been searching for.

Often called "lawsuit funding" or "litigation financing," this is a specialized service where companies like Flash Legal Funding provide a cash advance based on the strength of your case.

Here are the top five benefits that make pre-settlement funding a game-changer for plaintiffs:

## 1. Zero Upfront Costs or Monthly Payments

Unlike a traditional loan from a bank, pre-settlement funding does not require you to pay an application fee or make monthly installments. The funding is repaid in one lump sum directly from your settlement check *after* your case concludes. This means no impact on your monthly budget while you wait.

## 2. It Levels the Playing Field

This is arguably the most important benefit. Insurance companies have deep pockets, and they know you don't. They often drag out legal proceedings in hopes that financial desperation will force you to accept a low-ball settlement offer.

Funding gives you "holding power." With your living expenses covered, you can afford to reject those initial low offers and give your attorney the time they need to fight for the full value of your case.

## 3. Quick Access to Cash

Legal cases move slowly, but your life moves fast. When rent is due or medical bills are piling up, you cannot wait months for a court date. Flash Legal Funding is designed for speed. The application process is simple, and once approved, funds can often be transferred to your account in as little as 24 hours.

## 4. Covers Essential Living Expenses

Personal injury lawsuits can be financially draining, especially if your injury has forced you out of work. This funding is unrestricted, meaning you can use it for whatever you need most—whether that is rent, mortgage payments, groceries, car repairs, or medical co-pays.

## 5. No Credit Check Required

Your credit score is irrelevant to us. Pre-settlement funding is based entirely on the merits of your lawsuit. We evaluate the strength of your case, liability, and insurance coverage. Even if you have poor credit or are currently unemployed, you can still be eligible for funding.

## Conclusion

Pre-settlement funding is more than just cash; it is a strategic tool that empowers you to withstand the pressure of a long legal battle. It provides stability, protects your credit, and gives your attorney the time to win.

If financial stress is affecting your life while you wait for your settlement, consider reaching out to Flash Legal Funding to explore your options today.

This article is for general informational purposes. Always consult with your attorney to determine if pre-settlement funding is the right strategic move for your case.

# Broker vs. Direct Lender: Why Cutting Out the Middleman Saves You Money | Flash Legal Funding

> Don't pay extra fees to a middleman. Learn the critical difference between lawsuit funding brokers and direct lenders like Flash Legal Funding.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/posts/broker-vs-direct-lender/

By Flash Legal Funding Editorial Team · Published May 1, 2026

Funding Basics

# Broker vs. Direct Lender: Why Cutting Out the Middleman Saves You Money

Don't pay extra fees to a middleman. Learn the critical difference between lawsuit funding brokers and direct lenders like Flash Legal Funding.

May 01, 2026 • By Flash Legal Funding

When you apply for a lawsuit cash advance, you might assume you are dealing directly with the company that has the money. Unfortunately, that is not always the case.

The legal funding industry is split into two types of companies: **Direct Lenders** and **Brokers**. Knowing the difference can save you thousands of dollars and days of waiting time.

## What is a Broker?

A broker is a "middleman." They do not have their own capital to lend. Instead, they take your application, shop it around to multiple other companies (like ours), and try to find someone willing to fund your case.

While this might sound helpful, it often comes with hidden downsides:

- **Extra Fees:** Brokers need to get paid. They often add a "brokerage fee" on top of the loan, or they mark up the interest rate to take a cut.
- **Slower Process:** Because they have to send your file to third parties for approval, the process takes longer.
- **Data Privacy:** Your sensitive legal information is being shared with multiple companies, not just one.

## What is a Direct Lender?

A direct lender (or direct funder) is a company that has its own capital. They make their own decisions in-house.

**Flash Legal Funding is a direct lender.** When you apply with us:

- **We Review It:** Our in-house team looks at your case.
- **We Decide:** We don't need to ask permission from anyone else.
- **We Send the Check:** The money comes directly from us to you.

## Why Direct is Better

Cutting out the middleman usually results in **lower rates** because you aren't paying two companies (the broker AND the lender). It also results in **faster funding**. We can often approve and fund a case in 24 hours because the decision-makers are right here in the office.

## How to Spot a Broker

Brokers often have websites that look just like lenders. To find out, simply ask them: *"Are you funding this deal with your own money, or are you brokering it out?"*

If they hesitate, or if you see fees listed as "origination" or "brokerage" fees on your contract, you are likely dealing with a middleman.

## Conclusion

If you need cash fast and want to keep your costs as low as possible, go to the source. By working with a direct lender like Flash Legal Funding, you get transparent terms, faster answers, and no unnecessary middleman markups.

This article is for general informational purposes. We encourage you to ask any funding company if they are a direct lender before applying.

[← Back to all posts](/blog/)

### Need Pre-Settlement Funding?

No credit check. No monthly payments. If you lose your case, you owe $0.

[Apply Now — Takes 2 Minutes](/apply/)

Share this post

# Can I Get a Loan from My Attorney? Insights for Clients | Flash Legal Funding

> Explore the possibilities and legalities of getting a loan from your attorney and discover alternative solutions with Flash Legal Funding.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/posts/can-i-get-a-loan-from-my-attorney/

By Flash Legal Funding Editorial Team · Published February 20, 2026

Funding Basics

# Can I Get a Loan from My Attorney? Insights for Clients

Explore the possibilities and legalities of getting a loan from your attorney and discover alternative solutions with Flash Legal Funding.

February 20, 2026 • By Flash Legal Funding

## The Big Question: Loans from Attorneys

So, you're in a legal bind and wondering, "Can my attorney give me a financial lifeline?" Well, hold your horses! While it might seem like a quick fix, there are quite a few legal and ethical hoops to jump through. Most state bar associations frown upon attorneys loaning money to clients for personal expenses. Why? It's all about keeping things clear-cut and avoiding conflicts of interest. You wouldn't want your legal advice mixed with financial entanglements, right?

## Ethical Considerations: Keeping It Clean

Attorneys are bound by strict ethical rules. These rules are like the secret sauce that keeps the attorney-client relationship trustworthy. When it comes to loans, these guidelines are clear as day – no mixing financial assistance with legal services. It's like oil and water; they just don't mix well. This separation ensures that your attorney is 100% focused on your case, not on how you'll pay them back.

## Alternative Solutions: Flash Legal Funding to the Rescue

Now, don't get disheartened! While your attorney might not be your go-to lender, companies like Flash Legal Funding are like the superheroes of the legal financial world. They specialize in providing financial assistance to folks embroiled in legal battles. Whether you need funds to tide you over during a lawsuit or money to cover expenses, they've got your back.

### The Nuts and Bolts of Legal Funding

Legal funding isn't your typical loan. It's more like a cash advance against the potential settlement of your lawsuit. The cool part? It's non-recourse, which means if you don't win your case, you don't pay it back. Yep, you heard that right! It's a no-risk situation for you. Plus, the application process is usually straightforward, with no need to worry about credit checks or employment history.

### The Flash Legal Funding Advantage

Choosing Flash Legal Funding isn't just about getting financial help. It's about peace of mind. They understand the ins and outs of legal battles and offer funding that's tailored to your specific situation. Their team works like a well-oiled machine to ensure you get the support you need, fast and hassle-free.

## Smart Moves: Planning Your Financial Path

While Flash Legal Funding provides a fantastic safety net, it's always smart to plan ahead. Budgeting, exploring all your financial options, and discussing costs openly with your attorney can save you a lot of headaches down the road. Remember, knowledge is power, especially when it comes to your finances.

## FAQs: Quick Answers to Your Burning Questions

### Can my attorney lend me money for personal expenses?

Typically, no. It's against ethical guidelines in most jurisdictions.

### What's the difference between a loan and legal funding?

A loan requires repayment regardless of your lawsuit's outcome. Legal funding is a cash advance against a potential settlement, with repayment contingent on winning your case.

### How does Flash Legal Funding work?

They provide non-recourse cash advances against your potential settlement, meaning no payback if you don't win your case.

## Wrap-Up: Your Financial Ally in Legal Battles

In the end, it's clear that getting a loan from your attorney is a no-go. But hey, that's not the end of the road! Companies like Flash Legal Funding are here to fill that gap, offering a financial lifeline when you need it most. They're not just a funding source; they're your ally in navigating the choppy waters of legal finance.

This article is for general informational purposes and is not legal advice. Consult with your attorney regarding the specific ethical rules in your jurisdiction.

# How Does a Compounding Interest Rate Work? | Flash Legal Funding

> Compound interest acts like a snowball, turning a small loan into a large debt. Learn how the math works before you sign.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/posts/how-compounding-interest-works/

By Flash Legal Funding Editorial Team · Published April 10, 2026

Funding Basics

# How Does a Compounding Interest Rate Work?

Compound interest acts like a snowball, turning a small loan into a large debt. Learn how the math works before you sign.

April 10, 2026 • By Flash Legal Funding

When reviewing a lawsuit funding contract, you might see the term "compounding interest." While it sounds like a standard financial term, in the world of legal funding, it can be dangerous to your bottom line.

**Compound interest** occurs when interest is added to the original amount borrowed (the principal), and then the interest rate is applied to that *new, higher number* the following month. It is essentially "interest on interest."

## The Snowball Effect: A Real-World Example

Let’s look at how quickly this adds up. Imagine you borrow **$1,000** with a **2% interest rate that compounds monthly**.

- **Month 1:** You owe interest on the original $1,000. *Interest Cost: $20.00*
- **Month 2:** You now owe interest on $1,020 (the original $1,000 + last month's $20). *Interest Cost: $20.40*
- **Month 3:** You owe interest on $1,040.40. *Interest Cost: $20.81*

It might seem like small change at first (just 40 cents extra in Month 2), but legal cases often drag on for years. This "snowball effect" picks up speed over time.

### The One-Year Totals

At the end of the first year, you would owe **$1,268.24**—the initial $1,000 plus $268.24 in interest. If the case goes on for two or three years, that number grows exponentially, eating away at your final settlement.

## Compounding vs. Simple Interest

This is why it is critical to look for companies that offer **Simple Interest**. With simple interest, the rate is only ever calculated on the original $1,000 you borrowed.

At Flash Legal Funding, we believe in transparency. We don't want you surprised by a balance that has spiraled out of control. Always check your agreement to see if the word "compounding" is hiding in the fine print.

This article is for general informational purposes. Always ask a funding company to show you an amortization schedule (a chart of how fees grow over time) before signing.

[← Back to all posts](/blog/)

### Need Pre-Settlement Funding?

No credit check. No monthly payments. If you lose your case, you owe $0.
