# The Truth About 'Fixed Fees' in Lawsuit Funding | Flash Legal Funding

> Some lenders claim 0% interest but charge massive fixed fees. Learn how these multipliers work and why they can cost you thousands.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/posts/how-does-the-fixed-fee-work/

By Flash Legal Funding Editorial Team · Published March 30, 2026

Funding Basics

# The Truth About 'Fixed Fees' in Lawsuit Funding

Some lenders claim 0% interest but charge massive fixed fees. Learn how these multipliers work and why they can cost you thousands.

March 30, 2026 • By Flash Legal Funding

If you are shopping around for pre-settlement funding, you might see companies advertising **"No Interest Loans"** or **"0% Interest."** While this sounds like an amazing deal, there is almost always a catch.

Instead of interest, these lenders charge a "Fixed Fee" based on how long it takes your case to settle. Be careful: these fees often cost you significantly more than a standard interest rate would.

## How the Multiplier Works

Fixed fees usually work on a multiplier system. The lender will tell you that you owe a certain multiple of the loan amount depending on when you pay it back.

Let’s look at a real-world example of a **$1,000 advance** with a typical "1.5 Fixed Fee" structure:

- **0–6 Months:** You owe 1.5x the loan amount ($1,500).
- **7–12 Months:** The fee jumps to 1.8x ($1,800).
- **12+ Months:** The fee jumps again to 2.25x ($2,250).

## The Hidden Trap: The "Duration" Problem

The problem with this structure is that **you have no control over how long your lawsuit takes.**

If your case settles in month 7 instead of month 6, that one month of difference could cost you an extra $300 instantly. If your case drags on for over a year—which is very common in personal injury law—you could end up owing more than double what you borrowed.

## Predatory Tactics vs. Transparency

Some predatory lenders use fixed fees or compounding interest rates to obscure the true cost of the loan. They rely on the fact that "1.5" sounds like a small number, hoping you won't do the math to realize it effectively means a 50% interest rate in less than six months.

## The Flash Legal Funding Difference

At Flash Legal Funding, we believe in transparency. We don't hide behind confusing multipliers or hidden fee structures. We provide clear terms so you know exactly what you are getting into.

Before you sign any funding agreement, ask yourself: *"Do I know exactly how much this will cost if my case takes two years?"* If the answer is no, walk away.

This article is for general informational purposes. Always read the fine print of any funding agreement before signing.

This is where Flash Legal Funding comes in. We understand the importance of having financial stability during a legal battle, which is why we offer quick and easy funding solutions to help you cover your expenses until your case settles.

Here are some ways that Flash Legal Funding can help you succeed in your legal battle:

## 1. Quick Funding Solutions

At Flash Legal Funding, we understand that time is of the essence. That's why we provide our clients with quick and easy funding solutions. Our team of experts can provide funding within 24 hours of receiving your application, so you can cover your expenses and focus on your case.

## 2. No Credit Checks

Unlike traditional loans, Flash Legal Funding does not require a credit check. We understand that financial hardships can happen to anyone, and we don't want your credit score to hold you back from getting the funding you need.

## 3. Flexible Repayment Options

We understand that every case is unique, which is why we offer flexible repayment options. Our team of experts will work with you to develop a repayment plan that works for your specific case and financial situation.

## 4. Non-Recourse Funding

At Flash Legal Funding, we offer non-recourse funding. This means that if you don't win your case, you don't owe us anything. This takes away the risk of borrowing money during a legal battle.

## 5. Helps You Focus on Your Case

By providing you with financial stability, Flash Legal Funding allows you to focus on your case without worrying about financial burdens. This can help you succeed in your legal battle by allowing you to have a clear mind and the ability to make informed decisions.

## In Conclusion

Flash Legal Funding provides quick, easy, and flexible funding solutions to help you succeed in your legal battle. With no credit checks, flexible repayment options, and non-recourse funding, we take away the risk of borrowing money during a legal dispute. Contact us today to learn how we can help you succeed in your legal battle.

# How Pre-Settlement Funding Increases Case Value | Flash Legal Funding

> Stop settling for less because your client is desperate. See how funding gives you the leverage to fight for policy limits.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/posts/how-funding-increases-case-value/

By Flash Legal Funding Editorial Team · Published May 20, 2026

For Attorneys

# How Pre-Settlement Funding Increases Case Value

Stop settling for less because your client is desperate. See how funding gives you the leverage to fight for policy limits.

May 20, 2026 • By Flash Legal Funding

Every experienced attorney knows the scenario: You have a solid case with clear liability and significant damages. You know it’s worth the policy limits. But your client is out of work, their bills are piling up, and they are calling you every day asking, *"Why haven't we settled yet?"*

When a client is financially desperate, they lose the ability to be patient. They pressure you to accept the first low-ball offer the insurance adjuster throws on the table just to stop the bleeding. This is where pre-settlement funding becomes a critical strategic tool for your firm.

## The "Holding Power" Advantage

Insurance companies know that time is on their side. Their entire strategy is often built on Delay, Deny, Defend. They know that if they wait long enough, the plaintiff’s financial situation will deteriorate, forcing a cheaper settlement.

**Funding neutralizes this tactic.** By securing a cash advance for your client to cover immediate needs (rent, car payments, groceries), you buy yourself the "holding power" necessary to litigate the case properly.

## Better Treatment = Better Value

Financial stress often forces clients to miss medical appointments or skip necessary treatments because they cannot afford the copays or the gas money to get there. Gaps in treatment are the easiest way for defense counsel to devalue a claim.

Funding ensures your client can afford to continue their treatment plan, documenting their injuries fully and increasing the overall value of the case.

## A Win-Win for the Firm

Some attorneys hesitate to recommend funding because of the administrative burden or fear of high rates. At Flash Legal Funding, we have solved both:

- **Streamlined Process:** We require minimal documentation from your office. We respect your paralegals' time.
- **Fair Rates:** We offer competitive, simple interest options that ensure the client still walks away happy at the end of the case.

When clients are financially stable, they are patient. When they are patient, you can fight for the maximum value. And when case values go up, your firm's revenue goes up.

Partner with a funding company that understands litigation strategy. Contact Flash Legal Funding to learn about our attorney-preferred process.

# How Do Lawsuit Loans Work? A Comprehensive Guide | Flash Legal Funding

> Demystify the workings of lawsuit loans with our in-depth guide. Discover how Flash Legal Funding can support your legal journey.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/posts/how-funding-works/

By Flash Legal Funding Editorial Team · Published February 10, 2026

Funding Basics

# How Do Lawsuit Loans Work? A Comprehensive Guide

Demystify the workings of lawsuit loans with our in-depth guide. Discover how Flash Legal Funding can support your legal journey.

February 10, 2026 • By Flash Legal Funding

## Understanding the Basics

First things first, let's talk about what lawsuit loans are. They're not your typical loans. Instead, think of them as a cash advance against the future settlement or judgment of your lawsuit. It's like getting a piece of your potential settlement upfront. And the best part? You're not required to pay it back if you don't win your case.

## The Role of Flash Legal Funding

Now, where does Flash Legal Funding fit into this picture? They're like the guardians of your financial needs during legal proceedings. Flash Legal Funding specializes in providing lawsuit loans, helping you cover expenses while your case is still in the courts. They understand that legal battles can be long and costly, so they offer this financial cushion to ease your burden.

### The Application Process: Simple and Straightforward

Applying for a lawsuit loan with Flash Legal Funding is a breeze. You don't need to worry about your credit score or employment status. All they're interested in is the strength of your case. You simply fill out an application, they review your case details, and voilà – you could have the funds you need in no time.

### Calculating the Loan Amount

How much cash can you get? Well, it depends on your case. Flash Legal Funding evaluates the potential settlement or judgment amount and offers a percentage of that as a loan. They work closely with you and your attorney to determine a fair amount that can help you tide over without overwhelming you in the future.

### Repayment Terms: No Win, No Pay

Here's the kicker – if you don't win your case, you don't pay back the loan. Flash Legal Funding takes on the risk, offering non-recourse funding. This means they only get paid if you win or settle your case. It's a win-win situation for you.

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Is my credit score considered for a lawsuit loan?

Nope, Flash Legal Funding focuses on the strength of your case, not your credit score.

### How quickly can I get the funds?

With Flash Legal Funding, once your application is approved, you can get the funds swiftly, often within 24-48 hours.

### What if my case takes years to settle?

No worries! Flash Legal Funding works with you throughout the process, understanding the complexities of lengthy legal battles.

## Final Thoughts: Empowering Your Legal Journey

In a nutshell, lawsuit loans can be a lifeline during the uncertain times of a legal battle. Flash Legal Funding stands out as a trustworthy, client-focused provider, making sure you have the financial resources to fight your legal battle without added stress.

So, if you're entangled in a lawsuit and feeling the financial pinch, consider Flash Legal Funding as your go-to for lawsuit loans. They’re here to help you focus on what matters most – winning your case!

This article is for general informational purposes and is not legal advice. Consult with your attorney regarding funding options.

# How Will Interest Rates Be Calculated on a Lawsuit Loan? | Flash Legal Funding

> Confused by interest rates? We break down the difference between simple interest and fixed fees so you don't get overcharged.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/posts/how-interest-rates-are-calculated/

By Flash Legal Funding Editorial Team · Published April 5, 2026

Funding Basics

# How Will Interest Rates Be Calculated on a Lawsuit Loan?

Confused by interest rates? We break down the difference between simple interest and fixed fees so you don't get overcharged.

April 05, 2026 • By Flash Legal Funding

When you are looking for financial help during a lawsuit, the most important question to ask is often the most confusing one: **"How much will this cost me?"**

Most litigation funding companies charge interest on their lawsuit loans. However, some companies advertise "Zero Interest" and instead charge what they call a "Fixed Fee." While this might sound like a better deal, it is important to look closely at the details. A fixed fee structure often costs significantly more in the long run, leaving you with little to nothing after your case settles.

## The "Fixed Fee" Trap

Lenders who use fixed fees often charge based on time multipliers (e.g., owing 1.5x or 2x the loan amount). Because you cannot control how long your lawsuit takes, these fees can balloon rapidly, often outpacing standard interest rates.

## The Better Option: Simple Interest

The best lawsuit funding companies—including Flash Legal Funding—use a straightforward and **simple interest rate**. Unlike compounding interest (which charges interest on top of interest) or confusing multipliers, simple interest is calculated only on the original amount you borrowed.

This rate is generally determined on a case-by-case basis after a consultation with you and your lawyer. We consider:

- The probability of winning your case.
- The expected time until settlement.
- The potential settlement amount.

## Transparency is Non-Negotiable

Most reputable litigation funding companies do not charge a single, "one-size-fits-all" interest rate because every case carries a different level of risk. However, the rate should never be a mystery.

Before signing any official documents, it is crucial to understand the exact interest rate being charged. This information should be conveyed in clear, easy-to-understand language with absolutely no hidden fees.

At Flash Legal Funding, we believe you should never sign a contract you don't understand. We are always willing and able to answer any questions you have about the loan, the rate, and the application process.

This article is for general informational purposes. Always ask for a clear breakdown of the repayment schedule before accepting funds.

# How Much Pre-Settlement Funding Can I Get? | Flash Legal Funding

> Wondering how much cash you qualify for? Learn about the '10% Rule' and how we calculate a safe amount for your case.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/posts/how-much-funding-can-i-get/

By Flash Legal Funding Editorial Team · Published May 5, 2026

Funding Basics

# How Much Pre-Settlement Funding Can I Get?

Wondering how much cash you qualify for? Learn about the '10% Rule' and how we calculate a safe amount for your case.

May 05, 2026 • By Flash Legal Funding

When you are struggling with bills, the most important question on your mind is simple: **"How much cash can I get right now?"**

While every case is unique, there are specific guidelines that responsible funding companies follow. Understanding these numbers can help you set realistic expectations and plan your finances accordingly.

## The "10-20% Rule"

As a general industry standard, pre-settlement funding companies typically advance between **10% and 20%** of the estimated future value of your case.

For example:

- If your case is estimated to settle for **$50,000**, you might qualify for **$5,000 to $10,000**.
- If your case is estimated to settle for **$100,000**, you might qualify for **$10,000 to $20,000**.

## Why Can't I Borrow the Full Amount?

You might be wondering: *"If my case is worth $50,000, why can't I borrow $40,000?"*

The answer is that responsible lenders want to ensure you still walk away with money in your pocket when the case ends. Remember, your final settlement has to pay for:

- Attorney fees (usually 33%–40%).
- Medical liens and doctor bills.
- Repayment of the pre-settlement advance.

If a company lends you too much now, there might be nothing left for you later. We aim to strike a balance: giving you enough cash to cover your immediate needs (rent, car payments, food) without jeopardizing your final payout.

## How We Calculate Your Case Value

When you apply at Flash Legal Funding, our team looks at three main factors to determine your limit:

### 1. Liability (Who is at fault?)

Is it clear that the other party caused the accident? If liability is disputed, the risk is higher, and the funding amount may be lower.

### 2. Damages (How bad is the injury?)

We look at your medical records. Broken bones, surgeries, and long-term treatments generally indicate a higher case value than soft tissue injuries.

### 3. Insurance Coverage

Does the defendant have enough insurance to pay the claim? Even if you have severe injuries, we are limited by the insurance policy limits of the at-fault driver.

## Conclusion

While we can't tell you an exact dollar amount until we review your case, we promise to offer the maximum amount safe for your situation. Our goal is to be a lifeline, not a burden.

Ready to find out your specific number? Apply today for a free case evaluation. We can usually tell you how much you qualify for within 24 hours.

## Related Articles

- [How Do Lawsuit Loans Work? A Comprehensive Guide](/posts/how-funding-works/)
- [How Interest Rates Are Calculated for Pre-Settlement Funding](/posts/how-interest-rates-are-calculated/)
- [Top Tips for Getting Approved for Lawsuit Funding](/posts/tips-for-approval/)
- [Car Accident Pre-Settlement Funding](/car-accident-funding/)

# How to Choose the Right Lawsuit Funding Company for Your Needs | Flash Legal Funding

> Not all funding companies are created equal. Learn exactly what to look for—from reputation to fee structures—before you sign.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/posts/how-to-choose-the-right-company/

By Flash Legal Funding Editorial Team · Published March 15, 2026

Funding Basics

# How to Choose the Right Lawsuit Funding Company for Your Needs

Not all funding companies are created equal. Learn exactly what to look for—from reputation to fee structures—before you sign.

March 15, 2026 • By Flash Legal Funding

If you have been injured and are involved in a legal case, you may find yourself in a situation where you need financial assistance while waiting for your case to resolve. That is exactly where lawsuit funding comes in.

However, **not all lawsuit funding companies are created equal.** The industry has grown rapidly, and it is crucial to find a partner who puts your interests first. Here is a guide on how to choose the right lawsuit funding company for your specific needs.

## 1. Evaluate the Company's Reputation

Trust is the foundation of any financial relationship. Before you apply, look for online reviews and testimonials from past clients on platforms like Google or Trustpilot. Check if the company is a member of professional organizations or has industry accreditations. A strong track record is a good indicator that the company is reliable, ethical, and easy to work with.

## 2. Understand the Fees and Rates

While lawsuit funding is a valuable resource, you must understand the cost. Some companies use confusing jargon to hide high interest rates or compounding fees that can eat away at your settlement. Look for a company that offers competitive rates and, most importantly, **simple terms**. Never sign an agreement until you clearly understand how much you will owe when your case settles.

## 3. Demand Transparency and Communication

The funding process shouldn't be a mystery. Choose a company that communicates clearly and is willing to answer your questions without hesitation. If a company is evasive about their terms or pushes you to sign quickly without reading the fine print, walk away. A reputable funder like Flash Legal Funding will always walk you through the agreement step-by-step.

## 4. Assess Their Customer Service

You are likely going through a stressful time, and the last thing you need is a funding company that treats you like just another number. Look for a team that is responsive, empathetic, and provides personalized service. You want to feel comfortable calling them for updates and confident that they are handling your case with care.

## Conclusion

Choosing the right lawsuit funding company is a decision that impacts your financial future. Do not take it lightly. By considering reputation, transparency, fees, and service, you can make an informed choice.

At Flash Legal Funding, we pride ourselves on meeting all these criteria to provide you with the honest, fast financial assistance you need while you fight for justice.

This article is for general informational purposes. We encourage you to review all funding agreements with your attorney.

# How to Speed Up Your Lawsuit Funding Application | Flash Legal Funding

> Need cash fast? Here are the 3 simple steps you can take to ensure your funding request gets approved in 24 hours or less.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/posts/how-to-speed-up-application/

By Flash Legal Funding Editorial Team · Published May 10, 2026

Process

# How to Speed Up Your Lawsuit Funding Application

Need cash fast? Here are the 3 simple steps you can take to ensure your funding request gets approved in 24 hours or less.

May 10, 2026 • By Flash Legal Funding

When you are facing eviction, car repossession, or just a mountain of overdue bills, you don't have the luxury of time. You need funding **now**.

At Flash Legal Funding, we pride ourselves on speed—we can often fund cases in as little as 24 hours. However, the speed of the process often depends on one crucial factor: **cooperation**.

Here are three simple steps you can take to make sure your application flies through our system without delay.

## 1. Tell Your Attorney You Are Applying

This is the #1 reason for delays. Before we can give you money, we must verify the details of your case with your law firm. If your attorney doesn't know you applied, they might ignore our calls or emails, thinking we are spam.

**Action Step:** Before you fill out our form, call your law firm. Tell them: *"I am applying for funding with Flash Legal Funding. Please look out for their email and send them the requested documents."*

## 2. Have Your Basic Details Ready

Incomplete applications slow everything down. To process your request instantly, we need accurate information right from the start.

Make sure you have:

- Your attorney’s full name and phone number.
- The exact date of your accident.
- The type of accident (e.g., Motor Vehicle, Slip and Fall).
- A rough estimate of how much money you need.

## 3. Be Responsive

Once we approve your case, we will send you a contract to sign via email (usually DocuSign). The faster you sign it, the faster we can send the money. Keep an eye on your inbox (and your spam folder!) after you apply.

## Why It Matters

We are a direct lender, meaning we don't have to wait for outside approval. The money is ready to go. By preparing your attorney and checking your email, you remove the only roadblocks standing between you and your cash.

Need cash today? Call your attorney now, then click "Apply Now" to get the process started immediately.

# Is a Lawsuit Loan Actually a Loan? | Flash Legal Funding

> It is technically a non-recourse advance, not a loan. Learn the critical difference that protects you from debt if you lose your case.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/posts/is-a-lawsuit-loan-a-loan/

By Flash Legal Funding Editorial Team · Published April 20, 2026

Funding Basics

# Is a Lawsuit Loan Actually a Loan?

It is technically a non-recourse advance, not a loan. Learn the critical difference that protects you from debt if you lose your case.

April 20, 2026 • By Flash Legal Funding

It is one of the most common questions we get: **"Is a lawsuit loan technically a loan?"**

The short answer is **no**. While it functions like a loan in the sense that you receive money now to address immediate needs, the legal structure is entirely different. It is technically a **non-recourse cash advance** against your potential lawsuit settlement.

## The Crucial Difference: Repayment Risk

The difference between a "loan" and an "advance" might sound like semantics, but it has a massive impact on your financial safety.

- **A Traditional Loan:** You must pay it back, usually with interest, regardless of your personal circumstances. If you lose your job or encounter a financial crisis, the bank still expects payment.
- **A Non-Recourse Advance:** The repayment is strictly tied to the asset (your lawsuit). If the lawsuit fails, there is no asset to collect from.

This means if you lose your case and do not get a settlement, **you owe us nothing**. This "non-recourse" nature makes funding far safer and more appealing to clients than taking out personal debt or using credit cards.

## So, Why Do We Call It a "Lawsuit Loan"?

If it's not a loan, why do you see that term on our website?

The reality is that when people are in financial distress during a lawsuit, they search for what they know. Most people search Google for "lawsuit loans" or "loans for settlements."

We use the term simply because it is what most people associate with the service. We want to ensure that people looking for help can find us. However, once you apply, we make sure you understand that this is a **risk-free advance**, not a debt that will follow you around if your case doesn't go your way.

This article is for general informational purposes. We pride ourselves on clear contracts that explain the non-recourse nature of your funding.

[← Back to all posts](/blog/)

### Need Pre-Settlement Funding?

No credit check. No monthly payments. If you lose your case, you owe $0.

[Apply Now — Takes 2 Minutes](/apply/)

# Is a Lawsuit Loan Legal? Understanding the Legality of Litigation Financing | Flash Legal Funding

> A clear guide to the legality of litigation financing, state regulations, and how Flash Legal Funding ensures compliance.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/posts/is-a-lawsuit-loan-legal/

By Flash Legal Funding Editorial Team · Published February 15, 2026

Funding Basics

# Is a Lawsuit Loan Legal? Understanding the Legality of Litigation Financing

A clear guide to the legality of litigation financing, state regulations, and how Flash Legal Funding ensures compliance.

February 15, 2026 • By Flash Legal Funding

## The Legal Landscape of Lawsuit Loans

Alright, let's get down to brass tacks. Are lawsuit loans legal? In a nutshell, yes! These financial tools, also known as litigation financing or pre-settlement funding, are perfectly legal in most states. They provide a financial cushion to folks waiting for a lawsuit settlement. But, like everything legal, there are some complexities. Different states have different regulations, so it's not a one-size-fits-all scenario.

## How Lawsuit Loans Work: A Quick Overview

Here's the scoop on lawsuit loans. They're more like a cash advance against your future lawsuit settlement or judgment. Unlike traditional loans, they're contingent on the outcome of your case – if you don't win, you don't pay back. It's a lifeline for many who need financial support during lengthy legal battles.

## Flash Legal Funding: Your Legal Loan Guide

Enter Flash Legal Funding. These guys are pros in providing lawsuit loans. They're all about helping you navigate the legal funding waters while ensuring everything is above board. Flash Legal Funding tailors its services to meet your unique needs, offering financial support that aligns with legal requirements.

### The Fine Print: Understanding Terms and Conditions

Before you jump on the lawsuit loan bandwagon, it's crucial to understand the terms and conditions. Flash Legal Funding makes this easy. They're transparent about interest rates, repayment terms, and how they calculate funding amounts. Remember, knowledge is power, especially when it's about your finances.

### State Regulations: Navigating Different Rules

One key thing to remember is that lawsuit loan legality can vary by state. That's where Flash Legal Funding shines. They're well-versed in state-specific regulations, ensuring that your funding agreement is not just beneficial but also legally compliant.

## Making the Right Choice with Flash Legal Funding

Choosing Flash Legal Funding isn't just about getting cash. It's about making an informed, legally sound decision. They guide you through the process, ensuring that you understand every aspect of your lawsuit loan and how it fits within the legal framework of your state.

## FAQs: Clearing Up Your Concerns

### Are lawsuit loans legal in all states?

Mostly, yes, but regulations can vary. It's important to check the specific laws in your state.

### How does Flash Legal Funding ensure legal compliance?

They stay updated on state laws and customize their services to align with legal requirements.

### What happens if I lose my case?

With Flash Legal Funding, if you don't win your case, you don't owe them anything. It's that simple.

## Closing Thoughts: Navigating Lawsuit Loans with Confidence

So, there you have it! Lawsuit loans are indeed legal, and with Flash Legal Funding, you're in good hands. They ensure legal compliance, transparency, and tailor their services to your needs. If you're considering a lawsuit loan, remember to do your homework, understand the terms, and choose a trusted provider like Flash Legal Funding.

This article is for general informational purposes and is not legal advice. Consult with your attorney regarding funding options in your state.

No credit check. No monthly payments. If you lose your case, you owe $0.

# What Is the Process for Pre-Settlement Funding? | Flash Legal Funding

> Getting funding shouldn't be as hard as the lawsuit itself. Learn our simple 4-step process to get cash in as little as 24 hours.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/posts/process-for-pre-settlement-funding/

By Flash Legal Funding Editorial Team · Published April 15, 2026

Process

# What Is the Process for Pre-Settlement Funding?

Getting funding shouldn't be as hard as the lawsuit itself. Learn our simple 4-step process to get cash in as little as 24 hours.

April 15, 2026 • By Flash Legal Funding

Legal battles are complicated, but getting the money you need shouldn't be. At Flash Legal Funding, we have stripped away the red tape to create a streamlined, four-step process designed to get cash in your hands as quickly as possible.

Here is exactly how it works:

## Step 1: The Application

Start by giving us the basics: your contact information, the key details of your case, and your attorney's contact info.

**Tip:** Let your attorney know you are applying. Since we need to verify the case details with them, giving them a "heads up" can speed up the process significantly.

## Step 2: Expert Review

Once we have your application, our team of experts contacts your law firm to review the case details. We evaluate the strength of your claim to determine how much funding we can offer. Remember, **we do not check your credit score or employment history**. Our decision is based entirely on the merits of your lawsuit.

## Step 3: Approval & Contract

If you are eligible, we will send you an approval notice and a clear, transparent contract.

- Review the terms carefully (we pride ourselves on transparency).
- Sign the agreement.
- Your attorney will also sign a simple acknowledgement form.

## Step 4: Receive Your Funds

Once we receive the signed documents from you and your attorney, the request is processed immediately. We can send your funds via your preferred method—whether that is a check, bank transfer, or Western Union.

In many cases, funds are available within **24 hours** of approval.

## Ready to Get Started?

Don't let financial stress force you into a low settlement. Our process is fast, free to apply for, and completely non-recourse (if you lose, you pay nothing).

This article is for general informational purposes. Timelines may vary depending on how quickly your law firm can provide case documentation.

# Top Tips for Getting Approved for Lawsuit Funding | Flash Legal Funding

> Discover the key steps to increasing your chances of getting approved for lawsuit funding, from hiring an experienced attorney to demonstrating financial need.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/posts/tips-for-approval/

By Flash Legal Funding Editorial Team · Published March 1, 2026

Process

# Top Tips for Getting Approved for Lawsuit Funding

Discover the key steps to increasing your chances of getting approved for lawsuit funding, from hiring an experienced attorney to demonstrating financial need.

March 01, 2026 • By Flash Legal Funding

If you're in the middle of a lawsuit, you may be facing a financial crunch. The legal process can be long and expensive, leaving you struggling to cover basic expenses like rent, utilities, and medical bills. In such situations, lawsuit funding can be a lifeline. This is a type of funding that allows plaintiffs to borrow money against the potential settlement or judgment in their case.

However, getting approved for lawsuit funding can be a challenge. Here are some top tips to increase your chances of getting approved for lawsuit funding:

## 1. Hire an Experienced Attorney

One of the primary factors that lawsuit funding companies consider when reviewing funding applications is the strength of the case. A strong case means a higher likelihood of a favorable settlement or judgment, which increases the chances of repayment of the funding. To maximize the strength of your case, hire an experienced attorney who specializes in the type of law involved in your case.

## 2. Provide Complete and Accurate Information

When you apply for lawsuit funding, you'll need to provide detailed information about your case. This includes the type of case, the damages you're seeking, and the estimated timeline for the case to conclude. Providing complete and accurate information is critical because funding companies use this information to assess the strength of your case and the likelihood of repayment.

## 3. Demonstrate Financial Need

Lawsuit funding companies want to be sure that you have a legitimate financial need for the funding. This means that you'll need to demonstrate that you're struggling to cover your expenses due to the ongoing legal proceedings. Provide evidence of your financial need, such as bills, bank statements, and proof of income.

## 4. Be Prepared for a Thorough Review

Lawsuit funding companies conduct a thorough review of each funding application. They may request additional information about your case or ask to speak with your attorney. Be prepared to provide any additional information that may be requested and to answer any questions that the funding company may have.

## 5. Work with a Reputable Funding Company

There are many companies that offer lawsuit funding, but not all of them are reputable. Be sure to do your research and work with a company that has a good reputation in the industry. Look for companies that have positive reviews from past clients, transparent fee structures, and clear terms and conditions.

## Conclusion

Getting approved for lawsuit funding can be a great way to alleviate financial stress during a legal dispute. However, it's important to take the necessary steps to increase your chances of approval. By following these top tips, you'll be well on your way to securing the funding you need to cover your expenses while your case is ongoing.

This article is for general informational purposes and is not legal advice. Consult with your attorney regarding funding options in your state.

- [What Is the Process for Pre-Settlement Funding?](/posts/process-for-pre-settlement-funding/)

- [How Much Pre-Settlement Funding Can I Get?](/posts/how-much-funding-can-i-get/)
- [Broker vs. Direct Lender: What's the Difference?](/posts/broker-vs-direct-lender/)

# What Happens After You Are Approved? From Contract to Cash | Flash Legal Funding

> You've been approved! Now what? Learn about the final steps to get your cash, including digital contracts and payment options.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/posts/what-happens-after-approval/

By Flash Legal Funding Editorial Team · Published May 15, 2026

Process

# What Happens After You Are Approved? From Contract to Cash

You've been approved! Now what? Learn about the final steps to get your cash, including digital contracts and payment options.

May 15, 2026 • By Flash Legal Funding

Congratulations! You have applied, our team has reviewed your case with your attorney, and we have given you the "Thumbs Up." You are approved for funding.

But you don't have the money in your hand just yet. Many clients ask, *"What happens now?"* Here is a quick breakdown of the final steps to get your cash.

## 1. The Digital Contract

We don't do snail mail. As soon as you are approved, we will email you a secure link (usually via DocuSign) to your funding agreement.

You can review this document right on your phone or computer. It outlines the exact amount you are receiving and the repayment terms. Once you are happy with it, you simply tap to sign.

## 2. The Attorney Acknowledgement

This is a critical step that some clients overlook. Because the funding is repaid from your future settlement, **your attorney must also sign a document** acknowledging the funding.

They are not co-signing a loan—they are simply agreeing to pay Flash Legal Funding out of the settlement proceeds when the case wins. We will send this to them automatically, but a quick nudge from you can help speed this up!

## 3. Choosing Your Payment Method

Once both signatures are in, we release the funds immediately. We offer several ways to get your money, depending on how fast you need it:

- **Wire Transfer / Direct Deposit:** The fastest option. Funds usually appear in your bank account within 24 hours (sometimes the same day).
- **Overnight Check:** We can FedEx a physical check to your home or your attorney's office.
- **Western Union:** For smaller amounts or if you don't have a bank account, this can be a good option for immediate cash pickup.

## 4. Repayment (The Easy Part)

Once you have the cash, your part is done. You don't need to make monthly payments. You don't need to worry about interest dates. You simply focus on healing.

When your case settles months or years down the road, your attorney will handle the repayment directly with us out of the settlement check. You likely won't even have to write a check yourself.

Already approved? Check your email for your DocuSign link, or call us if you haven't received it yet!

# What Happens If You Lose Your Case After Getting Funding? | Flash Legal Funding

> Worried about repayment risks? Learn how non-recourse funding protects you if your lawsuit doesn't go the way you planned.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/posts/what-happens-if-you-lose/

By Flash Legal Funding Editorial Team · Published March 5, 2026

Funding Basics

# What Happens If You Lose Your Case After Getting Funding?

Worried about repayment risks? Learn how non-recourse funding protects you if your lawsuit doesn't go the way you planned.

March 05, 2026 • By Flash Legal Funding

It’s the number one question on every plaintiff’s mind: **“What if I take the money, but I lose my case?”**

Losing a lawsuit is stressful enough without the added burden of a loan hanging over your head. If you’ve already received funding to cover your rent or bills during the legal process, the idea of paying it back after a loss can be terrifying.

Here is the good news: If you obtained a **non-recourse** advance (like the ones we provide), you are generally not responsible for repaying a single dime if you lose.

## First, What Exactly is Pre-Settlement Funding?

Before we dive into the "what-ifs," let's clarify what this funding actually is. Pre-settlement funding isn't a traditional loan from a bank. It is a cash advance provided to plaintiffs to help cover living expenses and legal fees while they wait for their case to settle.

Unlike a credit card or a mortgage, this funding is tied strictly to the outcome of your case—not your credit score or your bank account.

## The "Non-Recourse" Safety Net

This is the most important term you need to know: **Non-Recourse**.

A non-recourse advance means that the funding company assumes all the risk. We are investing in the *potential* of your case, not lending money to *you* personally.

- **If you win:** We are repaid directly from the settlement proceeds.
- **If you lose:** You owe nothing. You keep the money you received, and the funding company takes the loss.

At Flash Legal Funding, all of our advances are non-recourse. If you receive a cash advance of $5,000 or $10,000 and the judge rules against you, that money is yours to keep. We absorb the loss so you don't have to.

## Final Thoughts

Legal battles are unpredictable. While your attorney fights hard to win, there are never any guarantees in court. That is why non-recourse funding is designed to protect you.

It provides peace of mind, knowing that even in the worst-case scenario—losing your lawsuit—you won’t be left with a mountain of debt. You can focus on your recovery and your case, without the financial stress weighing you down.

This article is for general informational purposes. Always check your specific funding agreement to confirm it is a non-recourse advance.

## Related Articles

- [Understanding the Benefits of Pre-Settlement Funding](/posts/benefits-of-pre-settlement-funding/)
- [How Do Lawsuit Loans Work? A Comprehensive Guide](/posts/how-funding-works/)
- [Is a Lawsuit Loan Really a Loan?](/posts/is-a-lawsuit-loan-a-loan/)
- [How Much Pre-Settlement Funding Can I Get?](/posts/how-much-funding-can-i-get/)

# What Is a Motor Vehicle Accident (MVA)? A Guide for Victims | Flash Legal Funding

> From defining an MVA to the immediate steps you must take after a crash, this guide covers everything you need to know to protect your rights.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/posts/what-is-a-mva/

By Flash Legal Funding Editorial Team · Published March 25, 2026

Legal Guide

# What Is a Motor Vehicle Accident (MVA)? A Guide for Victims

From defining an MVA to the immediate steps you must take after a crash, this guide covers everything you need to know to protect your rights.

March 25, 2026 • By Flash Legal Funding

A Motor Vehicle Accident, commonly referred to as an **MVA**, is a crash involving a vehicle such as a car, truck, motorcycle, or bus. These incidents can range from minor fender benders to catastrophic collisions resulting in life-altering injuries.

Regardless of the severity, understanding what happens immediately after an MVA is critical to protecting your physical health and your legal rights.

## Common Causes of MVAs

While every accident is unique, several common factors often contribute to collisions:

- **Distracted Driving:** Texting, eating, or using in-car entertainment systems.
- **Impaired Driving:** Operating a vehicle under the influence of drugs or alcohol.
- **Traffic Violations:** Speeding, running red lights, or failing to yield.
- **Environmental Conditions:** Poor weather, slick roads, or low visibility.

Regardless of the cause, the impact on your life—physical, emotional, and financial—can be significant.

## Immediate Steps to Take After an Accident

If you are involved in an MVA, remaining calm is the first challenge. Follow these steps to ensure you are protected:

### 1. Prioritize Safety and Medical Care

Check yourself and others for injuries. Even if you feel fine, adrenaline can mask pain. Seek medical attention immediately; a medical record is vital for any future insurance or legal claims.

### 2. Document the Scene

If it is safe to do so, take photos of the vehicles, the road conditions, and any visible injuries. Exchange contact and insurance information with the other driver(s), but **do not admit fault** at the scene.

### 3. Contact Law Enforcement

For any accident involving injury or significant damage, a police report is essential. This official document will be a cornerstone of your insurance claim.

### 4. Notify Your Insurance

Report the accident to your provider as soon as possible. They will begin the process of determining fault. If the other driver is responsible, their insurance should cover your damages, but this process can take time.

## The Financial Reality of MVAs

Here is the hard truth: **Settlements take time, but bills do not.**

If you have been injured and are unable to work, the financial pressure can build quickly. Medical expenses, car repairs, and regular monthly bills (like rent and utilities) can pile up while you wait for the insurance companies to negotiate.

This is where **Flash Legal Funding** steps in. If you need an immediate cash advance to cover your bills while your attorney fights for a fair settlement, we offer a straightforward solution.

Our application process is fast, we contact your lawyer directly to confirm case details, and we can often transfer funds within 24 hours. Don't let financial stress force you into a low settlement—let us help you bridge the gap.

This article is for general informational purposes. If you have been injured in an MVA, consult with a personal injury attorney immediately.

[← Back to all posts](/blog/)

### Need Pre-Settlement Funding?

No credit check. No monthly payments. If you lose your case, you owe $0.

[Apply Now — Takes 2 Minutes](/apply/)

Share this post

# Why Partner with a Funding Company? A Strategic Asset for Your Firm | Flash Legal Funding

> Client financial pressure is the enemy of maximum value. Learn how partnering with the right funding company buys you the time to litigate properly.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/posts/why-partner-with-us/

By Flash Legal Funding Editorial Team · Published April 25, 2026

For Attorneys

# Why Partner with a Funding Company? A Strategic Asset for Your Firm

Client financial pressure is the enemy of maximum value. Learn how partnering with the right funding company buys you the time to litigate properly.

April 25, 2026 • By Flash Legal Funding

As a personal injury attorney, your goal is to maximize the value of every case. But the biggest obstacle to a fair settlement often isn't the defense counsel or the insurance adjuster—it is **financial pressure on your own client.**

When a client is facing eviction, mounting medical bills, or vehicle repossession, they become desperate. They start calling your office daily, asking, *"When will my case settle?"* Eventually, they may force you to accept a low-ball offer just to stop the bleeding.

This is where partnering with a responsive, ethical funding company becomes a strategic asset for your firm.

## 1. Funding Buys You Time to Litigate

Insurance companies know exactly how to leverage delay tactics. They wait for the plaintiff's financial situation to deteriorate. By securing a pre-settlement advance for your client, you remove that leverage.

With their immediate needs covered, your client is no longer desperate. This gives you the breathing room to conduct thorough discovery, wait for MMI (Maximum Medical Improvement), and negotiate from a position of strength rather than necessity.

## 2. We Are Not "Loan Sharks"—We Are Partners

We know the reputation the funding industry has: excessive paperwork, interfering with case strategy, and predatory rates that make cases impossible to settle. **Flash Legal Funding operates differently.**

- **Minimal Interference:** We trust your judgment on liability and damages. We don't micromanage your case files.
- **Speed & Efficiency:** We know your paralegals are busy. Our process is streamlined to require minimal time from your staff.
- **Reasonable Rates:** We offer simple interest options that don't balloon out of control, ensuring there is still plenty of meat on the bone for the client (and you) at settlement.

## 3. Reducing Administrative Headaches

Nothing slows down a firm like a client in crisis. When clients are financially stable, they are easier to manage. They call less often about money and focus more on their recovery.

By referring clients to a trusted partner like Flash Legal Funding, you solve their immediate problem without your firm having to play banker or therapist.

## Conclusion

Pre-settlement funding is a tool. Used correctly, it protects your client's credit and empowers you to fight for the maximum policy limits. If you are looking for a funding partner that respects your time and your client's future, let's talk.

We prioritize attorney relationships. Contact us directly to discuss our streamlined review process for your firm.

# Privacy Policy | Flash Legal Funding

> Read the Flash Legal Funding privacy policy. Learn how we collect, use, and protect your information when you use our pre-settlement funding services.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/privacy-policy/

# Privacy Policy

This Privacy Policy describes how Flash Legal Funding (“we,” “us,” or “our”) collects, uses, and shares information when you use this website.

**Last updated:** February 2, 2026

## Information we collect

We may collect information that you submit to us, including:

- Contact details (such as name, phone number, and email address)
- Information you provide in messages or application forms
- Basic technical information (such as IP address, browser type, and device information)

## How we use information

We use information to:

- Respond to inquiries and communicate with you
- Review and process funding requests and applications
- Coordinate with your attorney or law firm (when applicable and authorized)
- Maintain, operate, and improve the website
- Detect, prevent, and address security issues and abuse

## Sharing of information

We may share information in limited circumstances, including:

- **With your attorney/law firm:** to evaluate and administer an application (as needed and with appropriate authorization)
- **Service providers:** vendors that help us operate the site (for example, hosting, form processing, or email delivery)
- **Legal requirements:** when we believe disclosure is required by law or necessary to protect rights, safety, or prevent fraud
- **Business transfers:** if we are involved in a merger, acquisition, or sale of assets, information may be transferred as part of that transaction

## Forms and submissions

If you submit information through an online form, your submission may be processed by a third-party form provider (depending on the form tool we use). We use those submissions only for responding to you and evaluating funding inquiries.

## Cookies and analytics

We may use cookies or similar technologies to operate the site and understand usage patterns. You can control cookies through your browser settings.

## Data retention

We retain information for as long as necessary to respond to inquiries, evaluate applications, comply with legal obligations, resolve disputes, and enforce agreements.

## Security

We take reasonable measures to protect information, but no method of transmission or storage is 100% secure. Please do not submit highly sensitive information unless necessary for your inquiry.

## Children’s privacy

This website is not intended for children under 13, and we do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13.

## Your choices

You may contact us to request access, correction, or deletion of information you have provided, subject to applicable law and legitimate business needs.

## Contact us

If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, contact us at [info@flashlegalfunding.com](mailto:info@flashlegalfunding.com) or call [(859) 81-FLASH](tel:+18598135274).

This Privacy Policy is provided for general informational purposes. If you have specific compliance questions (for example, California privacy requirements), consider consulting counsel.

> Read the Terms of Use for Flash Legal Funding. This agreement governs your use of the flashlegalfunding.com website and services.

Source: https://flashlegalfunding.com/terms-of-use/

# Terms of Use

Agreement between you and Flash Legal Funding (“FLF”) regarding your use of Flashlegalfunding.com (the “Site”).

**Effective as of:** February 17, 2023

## Agreement between User and Flashlegalfunding.com

Welcome to Flashlegalfunding.com. The Flashlegalfunding.com website (the "Site") is comprised of various web pages operated by Flash Legal Funding ("FLF"). Flashlegalfunding.com is offered to you conditioned on your acceptance without modification of the terms, conditions, and notices contained herein (the "Terms"). Your use of Flashlegalfunding.com constitutes your agreement to all such Terms. Please read these terms carefully, and keep a copy of them for your reference.

## Flashlegalfunding.com is a lawsuit financing site

Flashlegalfunding.com is a lawsuit financing site.

Flash Legal Funding provides financial support to individuals who have filed a lawsuit and are waiting for a settlement or judgment to be reached. Flash Legal Funding pays the claimant a portion of the expected settlement amount in advance, in exchange for a portion of the eventual settlement or judgment. This type of funding is often used by plaintiffs who need money to cover living expenses, medical bills, or other costs while they wait for their cases to be resolved.

## Electronic Communications

Visiting Flashlegalfunding.com or sending emails to FLF constitutes electronic communications. You consent to receive electronic communications and you agree that all agreements, notices, disclosures and other communications that we provide to you electronically, via email and on the Site, satisfy any legal requirement that such communications be in writing.

## Children Under Thirteen

FLF does not knowingly collect, either online or offline, personal information from persons under the age of thirteen. If you are under 18, you may use Flashlegalfunding.com only with permission of a parent or guardian.

## Links to Third Party Sites / Third Party Services

Flashlegalfunding.com may contain links to other websites ("Linked Sites"). The Linked Sites are not under the control of FLF and FLF is not responsible for the contents of any Linked Site, including without limitation any link contained in a Linked Site, or any changes or updates to a Linked Site. FLF is providing these links to you only as a convenience, and the inclusion of any link does not imply endorsement by FLF of the site or any association with its operators.

Certain services made available via Flashlegalfunding.com are delivered by third party sites and organizations. By using any product, service or functionality originating from the Flashlegalfunding.com domain, you hereby acknowledge and consent that FLF may share such information and data with any third party with whom FLF has a contractual relationship to provide the requested product, service or functionality on behalf of Flashlegalfunding.com users and customers.

## No Unlawful or Prohibited Use / Intellectual Property

You are granted a non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable license to access and use Flashlegalfunding.com strictly in accordance with these terms of use. As a condition of your use of the Site, you warrant to FLF that you will not use the Site for any purpose that is unlawful or prohibited by these Terms. You may not use the Site in any manner which could damage, disable, overburden, or impair the Site or interfere with any other party's use and enjoyment of the Site. You may not obtain or attempt to obtain any materials or information through any means not intentionally made available or provided for through the Site.

All content included as part of the Service, such as text, graphics, logos, images, as well as the compilation thereof, and any software used on the Site, is the property of FLF or its suppliers and protected by copyright and other laws that protect intellectual property and proprietary rights. You agree to observe and abide by all copyright and other proprietary notices, legends or other restrictions contained in any such content and will not make any changes thereto.

You will not modify, publish, transmit, reverse engineer, participate in the transfer or sale, create derivative works, or in any way exploit any of the content, in whole or in part, found on the Site. FLF content is not for resale. Your use of the Site does not entitle you to make any unauthorized use of any protected content, and in particular you will not delete or alter any proprietary rights or attribution notices in any content. You will use protected content solely for your personal use, and will make no other use of the content without the express written permission of FLF and the copyright owner. You agree that you do not acquire any ownership rights in any protected content. We do not grant you any licenses, express or implied, to the intellectual property of FLF or our licensors except as expressly authorized by these Terms.

## Use of Communication Services

The Site may contain bulletin board services, chat areas, news groups, forums, communities, personal web pages, calendars, and/or other message or communication facilities designed to enable you to communicate with the public at large or with a group (collectively, "Communication Services"). You agree to use the Communication Services only to post, send and receive messages and material that are proper and related to the particular Communication Service.

By way of example, and not as a limitation, you agree that when using a Communication Service, you will not:

- defame, abuse, harass, stalk, threaten or otherwise violate the legal rights (such as rights of privacy and publicity) of others;
- publish, post, upload, distribute or disseminate any inappropriate, profane, defamatory, infringing, obscene, indecent or unlawful topic, name, material or information;
- upload files that contain software or other material protected by intellectual property laws (or by rights of privacy of publicity) unless you own or control the rights thereto or have received all necessary consents;
- upload files that contain viruses, corrupted files, or any other similar software or programs that may damage the operation of another's computer;
- advertise or offer to sell or buy any goods or services for any business purpose, unless such Communication Service specifically allows such messages;
- conduct or forward surveys, contests, pyramid schemes or chain letters;
- download any file posted by another user of a Communication Service that you know, or reasonably should know, cannot be legally distributed in such manner;
- falsify or delete any author attributions, legal or other proper notices or proprietary designations or labels of the origin or source of software or other material contained in a file that is uploaded;
- restrict or inhibit any other user from using and enjoying the Communication Services;
- violate any code of conduct or other guidelines which may be applicable for any particular Communication Service;
- harvest or otherwise collect information about others, including e-mail addresses, without their consent;
- violate any applicable laws or regulations.

FLF has no obligation to monitor the Communication Services. However, FLF reserves the right to review materials posted to a Communication Service and to remove any materials in its sole discretion. FLF reserves the right to terminate your access to any or all of the Communication Services at any time without notice for any reason whatsoever.

FLF reserves the right at all times to disclose any information as necessary to satisfy any applicable law, regulation, legal process or governmental request, or to edit, refuse to post or to remove any information or materials, in whole or in part, in FLF's sole discretion.

Always use caution when giving out any personally identifying information about yourself or your children in any Communication Service. FLF does not control or endorse the content, messages or information found in any Communication Service and, therefore, FLF specifically disclaims any liability with regard to the Communication Services and any actions resulting from your participation in any Communication Service. Managers and hosts are not authorized FLF spokespersons, and their views do not necessarily reflect those of FLF.

Materials uploaded to a Communication Service may be subject to posted limitations on usage, reproduction and/or dissemination. You are responsible for adhering to such limitations if you upload the materials.

## Materials Provided to Flashlegalfunding.com or Posted on Any FLF Web Page

FLF does not claim ownership of the materials you provide to Flashlegalfunding.com (including feedback and suggestions) or post, upload, input or submit to any FLF Site or our associated services (collectively "Submissions"). However, by posting, uploading, inputting, providing or submitting your Submission you are granting FLF, our affiliated companies and necessary sublicensees permission to use your Submission in connection with the operation of their Internet businesses including, without limitation, the rights to: copy, distribute, transmit, publicly display, publicly perform, reproduce, edit, translate and reformat your Submission; and to publish your name in connection with your Submission.

No compensation will be paid with respect to the use of your Submission, as provided herein. FLF is under no obligation to post or use any Submission you may provide and may remove any Submission at any time in FLF's sole discretion.

By posting, uploading, inputting, providing or submitting your Submission you warrant and represent that you own or otherwise control all of the rights to your Submission as described in this section including, without limitation, all the rights necessary for you to provide, post, upload, input or submit the Submissions.

## International Users

The Service is controlled, operated and administered by FLF from our offices within the USA. If you access the Service from a location outside the USA, you are responsible for compliance with all local laws. You agree that you will not use the FLF Content accessed through Flashlegalfunding.com in any country or in any manner prohibited by any applicable laws, restrictions or regulations.

## Indemnification

You agree to indemnify, defend and hold harmless FLF, its officers, directors, employees, agents and third parties, for any losses, costs, liabilities and expenses (including reasonable attorney's fees) relating to or arising out of your use of or inability to use the Site or services, any user postings made by you, your violation of any terms of this Agreement or your violation of any rights of a third party, or your violation of any applicable laws, rules or regulations. FLF reserves the right, at its own cost, to assume the exclusive defense and control of any matter otherwise subject to indemnification by you, in which event you will fully cooperate with FLF in asserting any available defenses.

## Arbitration

In the event the parties are not able to resolve any dispute between them arising out of or concerning these Terms and Conditions, or any provisions hereof, whether in contract, tort, or otherwise at law or in equity for damages or any other relief, then such dispute shall be resolved only by final and binding arbitration pursuant to the Federal Arbitration Act, conducted by a single neutral arbitrator and administered by the American Arbitration Association, or a similar arbitration service selected by the parties, in a location mutually agreed upon by the parties. The arbitrator's award shall be final, and judgment may be entered upon it in any court having jurisdiction. In the event that any legal or equitable action, proceeding or arbitration arises out of or concerns these Terms and Conditions, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover its costs and reasonable attorney's fees. The parties agree to arbitrate all disputes and claims in regards to these Terms and Conditions or any disputes arising as a result of these Terms and Conditions, whether directly or indirectly, including Tort claims that are a result of these Terms and Conditions. The parties agree that the Federal Arbitration Act governs the interpretation and enforcement of this provision. The entire dispute, including the scope and enforceability of this arbitration provision shall be determined by the Arbitrator. This arbitration provision shall survive the termination of these Terms and Conditions.

THE INFORMATION, SOFTWARE, PRODUCTS, AND SERVICES INCLUDED IN OR AVAILABLE THROUGH THE SITE MAY INCLUDE INACCURACIES OR TYPOGRAPHICAL ERRORS. CHANGES ARE PERIODICALLY ADDED TO THE INFORMATION HEREIN. FLASH LEGAL FUNDING AND/OR ITS SUPPLIERS MAY MAKE IMPROVEMENTS AND/OR CHANGES IN THE SITE AT ANY TIME.

FLASH LEGAL FUNDING AND/OR ITS SUPPLIERS MAKE NO REPRESENTATIONS ABOUT THE SUITABILITY, RELIABILITY, AVAILABILITY, TIMELINESS, AND ACCURACY OF THE INFORMATION, SOFTWARE, PRODUCTS, SERVICES AND RELATED GRAPHICS CONTAINED ON THE SITE FOR ANY PURPOSE. TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, ALL SUCH INFORMATION, SOFTWARE, PRODUCTS, SERVICES AND RELATED GRAPHICS ARE PROVIDED "AS IS" WITHOUT WARRANTY OR CONDITION OF ANY KIND. FLASH LEGAL FUNDING AND/OR ITS SUPPLIERS HEREBY DISCLAIM ALL WARRANTIES AND CONDITIONS WITH REGARD TO THIS INFORMATION, SOFTWARE, PRODUCTS, SERVICES AND RELATED GRAPHICS, INCLUDING ALL IMPLIED WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, TITLE AND NON-INFRINGEMENT.

AT FLASH LEGAL FUNDING, WE PROVIDE INFORMATIVE BLOG POSTS THAT COVER VARIOUS LEGAL TOPICS RELATED TO PRE-SETTLEMENT FUNDING. HOWEVER, IT'S ESSENTIAL TO NOTE THAT OUR BLOG POSTS ARE NOT INTENDED TO PROVIDE LEGAL ADVICE OR REPRESENTATION. THE INFORMATION PROVIDED IS FOR EDUCATIONAL AND INFORMATIONAL PURPOSES ONLY AND SHOULD NOT BE RELIED UPON AS LEGAL ADVICE. IT'S ALSO IMPORTANT TO UNDERSTAND THAT THERE MAY BE ERRORS IN THE INFORMATION WE PROVIDE, AND WE ARE NOT RESPONSIBLE FOR ANY LEGAL DECISIONS OR OUTCOMES RELATED TO YOUR CASE. WE HIGHLY RECOMMEND SEEKING ADVICE FROM A QUALIFIED ATTORNEY BEFORE MAKING ANY LEGAL DECISIONS. OUR GOAL IS TO PROVIDE HELPFUL AND INFORMATIVE CONTENT TO OUR READERS, BUT WE ALWAYS RECOMMEND CONSULTING WITH A LEGAL PROFESSIONAL TO ENSURE THAT YOU HAVE THE MOST ACCURATE AND UP-TO-DATE INFORMATION.

TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, IN NO EVENT SHALL FLASH LEGAL FUNDING AND/OR ITS SUPPLIERS BE LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, PUNITIVE, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES OR ANY DAMAGES WHATSOEVER INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, DAMAGES FOR LOSS OF USE, DATA OR PROFITS, ARISING OUT OF OR IN ANY WAY CONNECTED WITH THE USE OR PERFORMANCE OF THE SITE, WITH THE DELAY OR INABILITY TO USE THE SITE OR RELATED SERVICES, THE PROVISION OF OR FAILURE TO PROVIDE SERVICES, OR FOR ANY INFORMATION, SOFTWARE, PRODUCTS, SERVICES AND RELATED GRAPHICS OBTAINED THROUGH THE SITE, OR OTHERWISE ARISING OUT OF THE USE OF THE SITE, WHETHER BASED ON CONTRACT, TORT, NEGLIGENCE, STRICT LIABILITY OR OTHERWISE, EVEN IF FLASH LEGAL FUNDING OR ANY OF ITS SUPPLIERS HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF DAMAGES. BECAUSE SOME STATES/JURISDICTIONS DO NOT ALLOW THE EXCLUSION OR LIMITATION OF LIABILITY FOR CONSEQUENTIAL OR INCIDENTAL DAMAGES, THE ABOVE LIMITATION MAY NOT APPLY TO YOU. IF YOU ARE DISSATISFIED WITH ANY PORTION OF THE SITE, OR WITH ANY OF THESE TERMS OF USE, YOUR SOLE AND EXCLUSIVE REMEDY IS TO DISCONTINUE USING THE SITE.

Use of the Site is unauthorized in any jurisdiction that does not give effect to all provisions of these Terms, including, without limitation, this section.

You agree that no joint venture, partnership, employment, or agency relationship exists between you and FLF as a result of this agreement or use of the Site. FLF's performance of this agreement is subject to existing laws and legal process, and nothing contained in this agreement is in derogation of FLF's right to comply with governmental, court and law enforcement requests or requirements relating to your use of the Site or information provided to or gathered by FLF with respect to such use. If any part of this agreement is determined to be invalid or unenforceable pursuant to applicable law including, but not limited to, the warranty disclaimers and liability limitations set forth above, then the invalid or unenforceable provision will be deemed superseded by a valid, enforceable provision that most closely matches the intent of the original provision and the remainder of the agreement shall continue in effect.

Unless otherwise specified herein, this agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the user and FLF with respect to the Site and it supersedes all prior or contemporaneous communications and proposals, whether electronic, oral or written, between the user and FLF with respect to the Site. A printed version of this agreement and of any notice given in electronic form shall be admissible in judicial or administrative proceedings based upon or relating to this agreement to the same extent and subject to the same conditions as other business documents and records originally generated and maintained in printed form.
